name: A Nosey Around Soho
description:
Step into the quirky and colourful world of Soho on this 90-minute guided walk, where history, art, and urban legends come to life. Discover secret passageways, uncover fascinating stories, and explore one of London’s most vibrant neighbourhoods.

What Can Customers Expect?
A Unique Urban Adventure – Soho is full of secrets, from secret alleyways to mysterious street art.

The Seven Noses of Soho – Will you spot all seven and unlock the legend of great wealth?

Street Art & Installations – Discover Soho’s ever-evolving art scene, from bold murals to tucked-away masterpieces.

Soho’s Architectural Secrets – Learn the stories behind famous build

name: Best of Blackfriars
description:
Step back in time and explore the fascinating past of Blackfriars, where Roman, Medieval, Tudor, and Victorian London intertwine. This 90-minute guided walking tour takes you through centuries of captivating stories, from Roman temples and Medieval monasteries to Tudor palaces and Victorian engineering marvels.

Follow in the footsteps of literary greats such as William Shakespeare, Samuel Pepys, and Charles Dickens. Stand on the site of a lost Tudor palace once gifted to Katharine of Aragon by Henry VIII and discover the remains of a medieval priory built by the Dominican Black Friars
highlights: A fascinating journey through time, exploring Blackfriars from its Roman origins to its modern transformation.
Engaging stories about historical figures, lost palaces, and secret landmarks that shaped London.
A knowledgeable guide bringing history to life with intriguing anecdotes and expert insights.
Opportunities to see both historic remains and cutting-edge architecture, showcasing the evolution of the area.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Blackfriars Station. Exact details will be provided upon booking.
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide. The tour starts promptly.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Toilets & Refreshments – Public toilets are available at Blackfriars Station.

name: Bleeding Hearts & Body Parts
description:
London’s history is steeped in blood, betrayal, and mystery. Join us on a journey through Farringdon and Smithfield, where the echoes of public executions, rebellions, and gruesome crimes still haunt the streets.

Beneath the historic streets of Farringdon and Smithfield lies a past stained with blood. This was a place of disease, rebellion, and religious turmoil—where martyrs were burned, criminals met their end, and the unfortunate simply disappeared. The cries of the condemned once rang out from execution sites, while plague pits swallowed thousands of nameless dead.

On this chilling journey, we peel back the layers of history to reveal London’s dark heart, where cold-blooded murder, public executions, and the dismembered remains o
highlights:
Time to take your rose-tinted history specs off.
Blood-soaked streets, shadowy alleys, and execution sites.
An Anglo-Saxon Princess – A tragic tale of power, exile, and an unmarked resting place. The area is linked to a princess whose story has faded into legend—along with the true location of her remains.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Farringdon Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your assigned guide.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.
Accessibility – The tour includes some very narrow walkways, steps and cobblestone. No Step-free route available. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Introduction & Welcome - A warm welcome and an overview of what to expect on the tour. Brief historical context of Farringdon and Smithfield.
Public Executions at Smithfield - Stand where London’s condemned met their fate. Learn about gruesome executions, from heretics burned at the stake to traitors hanged and quartered.

name: Pistol & Rifle Shooting Gallery
description:
Our purpose-built, covered Shooting Range offers 8 firing points, each fitted with safety barriers to ensure a secure and comfortable experience. Whatever the weather, you’ll stay warm and dry as you enjoy shooting activities under expert supervision.

The 6m Pistol Range accommodates up to 6 shooters with an automatic target retrieval system, while the impressive 40m Rifle Range features a wide variety of knockdown and reactive targets, including two exciting Duck Gallery Speed Shooting challenges.

We use powerful, easy-to-handle PCP pistols and rifles, with rifles fitted with telescopic sights for added accuracy

description:
Discover one of Oxford’s most traditional experiences on a private chauffeured punting tour along the River Cherwell.
This relaxing river journey offers a peaceful perspective of the city, passing Christ Church Meadows, historic college gardens, and some of Oxford’s most picturesque landscapes — with your punt reserved exclusively for your group.
Led by experienced chauffeurs, the tour combines gentle commentary with scenic views. Along the way, you will see:
Christ Church Meadows — linked to Lewis Carroll
Oxford Botanic Garden — Britain’s oldest botanic garden
Magdalen College School fields
St Hilda’s College gardens
The journey lasts approximately 30 minutes and returns to the Boathouse.

name: York: Historical Gems Tour & A Taste of York Chocolate
description:
Want to see the best of York? You are in for a cultural (and tasty) treat! Join your eccentric character guide and get an up-close walking tour experience of the city and its delightful hidden gems. York is a marvellously mysterious place, and you are going to experience it at its best. So, get ready; culture, history, heritage, and best kept secrets await on this character led walking tour.

You will pass by famous landmarks and have some of their surprising secrets revealed; Bettys, Bootham Bar and York Minster to name but three. You’ll learn the unknown truths of York’s most infamous characters Guy Fawkes and Dick Turpin. And York’s’ hidden gems? Well, you are in for many a surprise! Your savvy guide will point out treasures hidden in plain sight that even locals don’t know about; concealed courtyards, unusual statues, secluded snickelways, and curious artefacts that are so often simply passed by. Every gate (that’s a street!) has its own unique story to tell, and you’

name: Power, Profit & Progress Tour: Women in the City
description:
This engaging and thought-provoking tour takes you through the historic streets of the City of London, revealing the untold histories of extraordinary women who challenged expectations and left a lasting legacy.

We explore the lives of pioneering suffragettes, groundbreaking businesswomen, and unsung heroines who played crucial roles in shaping the city. From the fearless activists who fought for women's rights to the entrepreneurs and innovators who defied the odds, their stories will come to life as we visit key sites linked to their incredible achievements.

What Can Customers Expect?

This walking tour brings to life the remarkable stories of pioneering women who helped shape the City of London. - Through captivating storytelling and expert guidance, you'll gain insight into the struggles, achievements, and lasting legacies of these trailblazers. Expect to:
Walk through historic streets and lesser-known alleys, uncovering untold histories.
Learn

name: Fleet Street: a Private Tour
description:
Step into the world of ink and intrigue on Fleet Street, once the heart of British journalism. For centuries, this bustling thoroughfare was home to the country’s most influential newspapers, where headlines were crafted, scandals exposed, and history recorded.

On this captivating walking tour, we’ll uncover the stories behind Fleet Street’s rise and fall, exploring its legacy as the epicentre of news and publishing.

Our journey begins at the magnificent St Paul’s Cathedral, an enduring symbol of London’s resilience and a silent witness to the evolution of the press. From there, we’ll meander through the storied alleys and grand buildings that once housed Britain’s most powerful newspapers.

What Can Customers Expect?

A fascinating journey through the heart of London’s historic press district, uncovering the secrets of Fleet Street.
Engaging stories about press barons, scandalous headlines, and the evolution of journalism from its early days to the modern er
highlights: A fascinating journey through the heart of London’s historic press district, uncovering the secrets of Fleet Street.
Engaging stories about press barons, scandalous headlines, and the evolution of journalism from its early days to the modern era.
A chance to explore secret corners, historic pubs, and grand buildings once home to some of the most influential newspapers in the world.
Meeting Point – Arrive at St Paul's Tube Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Gough Square – Visit the former home of Dr Samuel Johnson, whose work shaped the English language and influenced generations of writers and journalists.
Stationers’ Court – Explore the historic base of the Worshipful Company of Stationers, which controlled the early printing industry and played a crucial role in media regulation.

name: Secrets of Spitalfields Tour
description:
Stories of Migration, Resilience, and Change

Step into the heart of Spitalfields, where centuries of migration, industry, and activism have left an indelible mark on London’s East End. This 90-minute walking tour reveals the untold stories of the people—especially the women—who shaped this dynamic neighbourhood.

Led by a qualified London tour guide, you’ll uncover tales of charity founders, workers’ rights activists, and social reformers whose contributions have long been overshadowed by history.

What Can Customers Expect?

Stroll down one of London’s most photogenic streets, where elegant Georgian townhouses stand as a testament to the Huguenot silk weavers who fled religious persecution in the 17th century. These ‘refugees’ not only shaped the area but also left their mark on the English language itself.

Uncover the secrets of Spitalfields’ entertainment past, from a historic music hall to the inspiring efforts of Annie Besant, who stood alongside the striking ‘match girls’ in a fight for fair pay and working conditions—an act that influenced the modern trade union movement.

Wander through Brick Lane, where street art and the ar
highlights: This isn’t just a walk through history—it’s a journey through the lives of those who fought for change, built new futures, and turned Spitalfields into one of London’s most fascinating districts.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Liverpool Street Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide, Bex Couper.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.
Accessibility – Not Step-free The route involves cobblestones. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Introduction – Explore how migration, activism, and industry have transformed Spitalfields over centuries.
Historic Landmarks – Visit a Grade II-listed synagogue, see the 17th-century Huguenot silk weavers’ houses, and discover a historic music hall.
Stories of Activism & Resistance – Learn about Annie Besant and the match girls’ strike, a pivotal moment in British workers’ rights.

description:
Embark on A Road to Ruin Walk: Londoners and Their Alcohol, a guided journey through London's historic streets, uncovering tales of vice, virtue, and the city's drinking culture.

A Road to Ruin: Exploring London's History with Alcohol and Art

Join us for a unique journey into the heart of 18th-century London, where the art of brewing and the culture of drinking shaped society and fuelled a lasting love affair with alcohol. Ideal for history enthusiasts and those with a passion for London’s colourful past, this guided walk reveals the intricate world of gin, beer, and social status as we explore Clerkenwell—a historic hub of breweries and gin distilleries.

Travel back in time and uncover the complex relationship between London’s different social classes and

description:
Overview
🎄 Discover London at its most enchanting on a private guided Christmas lights tour. Wander through the city’s most elegant streets, aglow with festive splendour, and explore the stories behind the sparkle with your own qualified guide. name: Private Great Fire of London Tour
description:
Step back in time to one of the most dramatic moments in London’s history: the Great Fire of 1666. On this captivating guided walk, you will follow in the footsteps of Samuel Pepys and others who witnessed the destruction of three-quarters of the city, forever altering its landscape.

Led by a London tour guide, this tour takes you through the narrow streets and historic landmarks that bore witness to the inferno. Learn how the fire began in a humble bakery on Pudding Lane before quickly engulfing churches, homes, and businesses. Discover the panic, heroism, and devastation that unfolded over four days as the City burned, and find out who was blamed for the disaster.

This tour also explores the lasting impact of the fire, from the introduction of new building regulations to the reconstruction of St Paul’s Cathedral under Sir Christopher Wren. Hear fascinating tales of survival, loss, and resilience, and uncover how some Londoners took advantage of the chaos for their own gain.

Perfect for history lovers and curious explorers alike, this immersive experience will bring the Great Fire to life through expert storytelling, little-known facts,
highlights: A walking tour led by a London tour guide, bringing history to life through expert storytelling.
Exploration of key sites related to the Great Fire, including Pudding Lane, where the fire started.
Fascinating insights into how the fire spread, who was blamed, and how Londoners responded.
The chance to follow in the footsteps of Samuel Pepys and other eyewitnesses.
Discover how the disaster led to new building regulations and the rebuilding of St Paul’s Cathedral.
An engaging and educational experience suitable for history lovers of all ages.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Monument Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide.

name: Private Secret Spaces in the City
description:
Think you know the City of London? Think again. Beyond the towering glass and steel lies a world of secret gardens, historic courtyards, and peaceful green spaces, waiting to be discovered.

Join Susan, your London tour guide, as she leads you through some of the most unexpected and beautiful secret spaces in London’s financial district. Discover how bombsites became tranquil gardens, explore London’s oldest park, and find out about a disappearing church.

This tour reveals the City’s unexpected greenery, telling the stories of how war, fire, and modern planning have shaped these secret oases. Along the way, you’ll also catch glimpses of Roman ruins from a unique perspective.

What Can Customers
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at 118 London Wall. (exact meeting details provided upon booking).

Explore Secret Gardens – Step into secret green spaces tucked away behind the City’s modern skyscrapers.

name: Quad Bike Trekking
description:
Experience the thrill of riding our powerful all-terrain quad bikes across 100 acres of varied and challenging countryside. Your trek will take you over steep hills, through open grassland, along woodland trails and, when the weather allows, across water splashes and through plenty of mud for that extra dose of excitement!

Each trek is led by an experienced instructor who will guide up to six riders at a time. Whether you’re booking as a full group or coming along on your own, we can easily combine individuals and smaller groups so everyone gets to enjoy the adventure.

Please note: Muddy sections and water splashes are dependent on the weather and cannot always be guaranteed

name: All Change at Kings Cross: Private Tour
description:
Step into the heart of one of London’s most dramatic urban transformations. Join us as we uncover the hidden stories behind this ever-evolving district.

What Can Customers Expect?

An insightful journey through King’s Cross, tracing its transformation from an industrial hub to a vibrant cultural district.

name: The Beatles walk through Liverpool
description:
Step back in time and follow in the footsteps of John, Paul, George, and Ringo as you explore central Liverpool – the city where it all began. This guided Walking Tour of The Beatles takes you to key locations that shaped the Fab Four's early lives and careers, blending history, music, and storytelling in the heart of the city. From the present to the past and (almost) everything in between!
We visit the Cavern Club, Mathew Street, Liverpool Beatles Museum, Cilla Black Statue, Eleanor Rigby Statue, Brian Epstein Statue and more with stories at all of the stops and weave a tale about the most famous band in the world!

name: Tate Modern Museum Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Discover the Tate Modern, London’s premier museum of contemporary and modern art, home to a world-class collection that spans the revolutionary movements of the 20th century. From Marcel Duchamp’s era-defining Fountain to the pop icons of Andy Warhol, this small-group tour—limited to just eight guests—explores the boldest artistic innovations of the modern era.

Housed in a former power station, the Tate Modern’s industrial architecture is as striking as the works it contains. The vast Turbine Hall, once home to towering generators, now hosts large-scale installations that redefine the boundaries of art. Discuss the Spanish Civil war before works by Picasso and Dalí, before experiencing the abstract expressionism of Rothko’s Seagram Murals and Jackson Pollock’s drip paintings. Step into the hypnotic world of Yayoi Kusama’s infinity installations, where mirrored surfaces and endless reflections blur the line between reality and illusion.

Led by an ex

name: Heretics and Horrors: a Private Tour
description:
Step into the shadows of London’s past, where the City was gripped by fear, fire, and fanaticism. This atmospheric tour takes you through the streets where heretics met their fate, the Great Plague ravaged the population, and traitors' heads adorned the city gates.

London has endured centuries of horror—whether through the terrifying flames of the Great Fire of 1666, the brutal reigns of Henry VIII and Bloody Mary, or the living conditions within London's first prison. Walk in the footsteps of those who lived (and died) through these dark times and uncover the secrets lurking beneath the city's grand exterior.

What Can Customers Expect?

Walk the streets devastated by the Great Fire of London and uncover its dramatic aftermath.
Learn about Henry VIII’s brutal Dissolution of the Monasteries and its lasting impact.
Discover the gruesome punishments and public executions that once defined justice in London.

Heretics and Horrors City Tour Reviews
Vicky (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
"An enjoyable and informative tour, our guide Hazel was interesting and knowledgeable and able to answer additional questions from the group. I will be looking to se
highlights: Walk the streets devastated by the Great Fire of London and uncover its dramatic aftermath.
Learn about Henry VIII’s brutal Dissolution of the Monasteries and its lasting impact.
Discover the gruesome punishments and public executions that once defined justice in London.
Meeting Point – Arrive at St Paul’s Cathedral (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide.
Introduction to London’s Dark Past – Set the scene for a journey through centuries of horror, persecution, and resilience.
The Great Plague & Great Fire of London – Walk the streets where disease spread and flames consumed the city, reshaping it forever.
Religious Persecution & Bloody Mary – Discover how heretics met their fate and why Mary I’s reign remains infamous.

name: Samuel Pepys's London: a Private Tour
description:
Samuel Pepys was more than just a diarist—he was a keen observer of 17th-century London, a naval administrator, and a man who lived through some of the city's most dramatic events. His vivid accounts provide an unparalleled glimpse into the Restoration era, covering everything from royal scandals and public executions to the Great Plague and the Great Fire.

What Can Customers Expect?

Step into Pepys’ London – Explore the city through the eyes of one of its most famous chroniclers.

Engaging Storytelling – Experience Pepys’ world brought to life by an expert guide, complete with entertaining anecdotes and historical insights.

Historic Landmarks – Walk through streets Pepys knew, visiting key locations tied to his life, including hi
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at Tower Hill. (exact meeting details provided upon booking).

Toilets & Refreshments – There are public toilet facilities located near,, in the underpass connecting Bridge Street and Parliament Street.

The Great Fire of London – Visit locations associated with the fire and hear Pepys’ firsthand account of the disaster.
Pepys’ Home and Workplace – Explore the sites where he lived and worked, including his naval office.

name: City Highlights: a Private Tour
description:
Explore Two Millennia of London’s History in the Square Mile

Step into the heart of the City of London and uncover the extraordinary stories woven into its streets, buildings, and traditions. This walking tour offers a captivating introduction to the Square Mile—where Roman foundations meet modern skylines, and every corner has a tale to tell.

Led by an experienced City of London guide, this tour brings to life the events, people, and places that have shaped the capital over two thousand years. Whether you're a first-time visitor or a long-time Londoner, you’ll discover how much history lies beneath your feet.

As you navigate the City’s winding alleyways and grand thoroughfares, you’ll explore how London has risen from the ashes of the Great Fire of 1666 and rebuilt itself after the devastation of the Blitz. You’ll hear how its resilience, adaptability, and sense of tradition continue to define it today.

More than just a financial powerhouse, the City of London is a unique blend of governance, heritage, and ceremonial customs. Learn about its ancient systems of authority, the symbolic role of the Lord Mayor, and the enduring influence of the City of London Corporati
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at St Paul's tube station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Accessibility –The route is not-step free. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.

Introduction at the Meeting Point
Meet your guide and fellow walkers, and hear a short introduction to the City of London—its geography, governance, and historical significance.

St Paul’s Cathedral
Begin with London’s most iconic cathedral, learning how Wren’s masterpiece became a symbol of resilience through fire, war, and renewal.

The Bank of England & Royal Exchange
Discover how the City became a financial powerhouse, explore its central institutions, and hear tales of economic transformation.

London Mithraeum
Step into Roman Londinium with a visit to the reconstructed Temple of Mithras—an archaeological gem revealing London’s ancient roots.

Experience the world's most famous prehistoric site in a manner that is meaningful, and truly unforgettable!

name: Join a Goat & Sheep Walk
description:
Enjoy a leisurely stroll with our friendly and playful goats through the stunning Kent countryside. Either myself or Mark will introduce you to Custard, Mustard, Carrot, Parsnip, Yin, Sprite, Biscoff & Banoffee our fabulous goats, and Kerbie & Klaus, our Valais Black Nose Sheep. Experience their unique personalities as you walk, brush, and bond with them. Perfect for nature lovers and families looking for a unique countryside experience.

Guided goat walk in a picturesque wild valley.

Opportunity to brush the goats and spend time with them in their field.

name: Georgian London Tour
description:
Explore Georgian London: Clerkenwell

Overview
Step into the elegance and intrigue of Georgian London with this captivating walking private tour, led by a qualified tour guide Hazel. Discover grand townhouses, alleyways, and the radical figures who shaped the area.

Step into Georgian London, a time of elegance, innovation, and lively social change. While the Georgian period is often remembered for refinement and grandeur, it was also an era of contrasts—where high society mingled with radicals, and entertainment thrived in unexpected places.

Led by a qualified London Tour guide, this tour explores North Clerkenwell’s past, tracing its transformation from a lively and rowdy district to a desirable neighbourhood. Discover how its streets, architecture, and local character have evolved over time.

W
highlights: Visit the historic site of Sadler’s Wells, a theatre with a scandalous past.
Uncover tales of radicals, entertainers, and engineers who shaped Georgian London.
Admire striking examples of Georgian architecture and town planning.
Explore the secret corners of Clerkenwell where Londoners came to work, play, and be entertained.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Angel Tube Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide, Hazel.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Accessibility – Not step-free. The route involves cobblestones, stairs (with handrail) and uneven surfaces. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.

name: Oliver Twist: a Private Tour
description:
Overview
Step into Victorian London, where crime, poverty, and survival shaped the daily lives of thousands. Inspired by Charles Dickens’ Oliver Twist, this immersive walking tour takes you through the very streets where famous literary characters Oliver Twist and the Artful Dodger once roamed, dodging the law and navigating the underworld of 1837 London.

Experience the streets of Dickensian London, where wealth and poverty existed side by side, and survival often depended on wit and cunning. This private tour takes you through the same alleys and marketplaces where Oliver Twist and the Artful Dodger made their way to Fagin’s lair.

What Can Customers Expect?
🔹 A Journey Through Time – Step back into 1837 and experience London as it was in Dickens’
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at Angel Tube Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide, Hazel.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Accessibility – Step-free route – fully accessible. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Toilets & Refreshments – Toilets are available in the basement of The Peacock Pub, located across the road from Angel Tube Station, as well as at Farringdon Station.
Introduction & Setting the Scene - Meet your guide and fellow participants. Learn about Charles Dickens, his connection to London, and the real-life inspiration behind Oliver Twist.
Exploring Dickensian London - Walk through Clerkenwell, a district filled with stories of crime, workhouses, and rebellion. Discover locations that reflect the dark underbelly of Victorian London.
Tour Conclusion & Recommendations - End Point: Near Farringdon Station.

name: Hampstead Village: Private Tour
description:
Step into one of London’s most picturesque and historic neighbourhoods on this immersive guided walk. Hampstead Village has long been a haven for artists, writers, and intellectuals drawn to its village charm, elegant architecture, and stunning green spaces.

For centuries, Hampstead has inspired some of the greatest minds in history. John Keats wrote Ode to a Nightingale here, landscape artist John Constable captured the beauty of the Heath, and Dracula author Bram Stoker found eerie inspiration in its atmospheric streets. The area was also home to renowned portraitist George Romney and countless other creatives.

Join a London tour guide as we explore Hampstead’s history, its famous blue plaques, hidden alleys, and the breathtaking H
highlights: A journey through history – Learn about the famous residents who shaped Hampstead’s legacy, from poets and painters to novelists and actors.
An immersive walking experience – Stroll through quaint streets, secret corners, and historic squares that tell the story of Hampstead’s evolution.
Breathtaking views – Admire the sweeping landscapes of Hampstead Heath, a timeless inspiration for artists and nature lovers alike.
A touch of culture and charm – Explore the independent boutiques, cafés, and historic pubs that make Hampstead feel like a village in the heart of London.
Expert storytelling – Your knowledgeable guide will bring Hampstead’s past to life with captivating stories and fascinating insights.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Hampstead Tube Station (exact details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide. The tour leaves promptly.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.

name: Southwark: a Private Tour
description:
Beyond the bustling stalls of Borough Market lies a fascinating district packed with stories of trade, theatre, and resilience. Southwark has long been a gateway to London, a place where medieval pilgrims gathered, Shakespearean theatre thrived, and some of the city’s most intriguing figures left their mark.

This 90-minute walking tour uncovers untold histories, literary connections, and remnants of the past, revealing the area's significance beyond its modern-day vibrancy. Discover how Southwark became the entertainment hub of Tudor London, with its playhouses, taverns, and bear-baiting pits drawing crowds from both sides of the river. Hear how Shakespeare found inspiration here, creating works that would define En
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at London Bridge Station(exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide, Hazel.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.
Accessibility – Step free and wheelchair accessible but involves cobblestones. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Toilets & Refreshments – Public toilets are available at London Bridge Station and Borough Market. You may wish to explore Borough Market before or After the Walk for food and drinks.
Introduction – A warm welcome and an overview of Southwark’s 2,000-year history.
Untold Histories of Southwark – Discover the lesser-known stories behind this fascinating area.
Literary Connections – Hear about Chaucer, Dickens, and Shakespeare, and their links to Southwark.

name: Royal London: a Private Tour
description:
Led by an expert guide, this tour takes you on a journey from medieval times to the present day, unveiling fascinating tales of power, scandal, and tradition. Learn about a deposed queen, the ancient art of falconry, and even some rather scandalous royal furniture!

Stroll through St. James’s Park, once Henry VIII’s hunting ground, later transformed into a duelling hotspot and home to James I’s menagerie and mulberry garden. Wander the historic courtyards of St. James’s Palace, where kings and queens have walked for generations, and uncover key moments that shaped England’s royal legacy.

Admire Clarence House, the official London residence of the Prince of Wales and former home of Queen Elizabeth II, be

St. James’s Park – Discover its fascinating past, from royal hunting grounds to duelling hotspots.

name: Private A Victorian Christmas
description:
Step into the festive charm of Victorian London and uncover the traditions that shaped the Christmas we know today. This immersive walking tour brings to life the sights, sounds, and stories of Christmas past, from the influence of Charles Dickens to the grand celebrations that transformed the holiday.

Wander through atmospheric streets and alleyways, learning how Christmas evolved from a little-celebrated occasion into a major event filled with feasting, gift-giving, and festive cheer.

Discover the origins of beloved traditions, such as Christmas trees, crackers, and carol singing, and see how Londoners of the past embraced the festive season.

Led by a knowledgeable guide, this 90-minute walking tour is a must for history lovers
highlights: A festive and immersive experience exploring the rich traditions of a Victorian Christmas.

name: Private Financial London Tour
description:
Explore London’s Financial Legacy
Step into the heart of the City of London, where fortunes have been made—and lost—for centuries. This guided walk takes you through the narrow alleys and grand institutions that helped shape London into the world's leading financial centre.

Discover the origins of the stock market, insurance, and banking, and see how these industries continue to drive the UK economy today.

name: Private Roman London Tour
description:
Step back in time and uncover the origins of London as we explore the heart of the Roman city. This immersive 90-minute walking tour reveals both grand remains and lesser-seen fragments of Londinium that most people never notice.

Discover why the Romans built their city here, meet some of its earliest inhabitants, and learn how this once-thriving metropolis was nearly abandoned. Marvel at the sheer scale of Roman London, from monumental ruins to subtle clues etched into the modern landscape.

What Can Customers Expect?

A fascinating journey through the Roman roots of London, exploring both well-known landmarks and hidden archaeological gems.
Engaging storytelling from a knowledgeable guide, bringing the daily life of Roman Londoners to life.
A mix of standing discussions and gentle walking, covering key sites such as the Roman Wall, the site of the Roman Forum, and the Amphitheatre remains.

description:
Step into the heart of British history with our Wonders of Whitehall walking tour. Explore one of London’s most historic districts, where royal intrigue, political drama, and world-famous architecture come together.

From the splendour of the Houses of Parliament and Westminster Abbey to the remnants of Henry VIII’s lost Tudor palace, uncover the untold stories behind these iconic landmarks.

name: Regent's Canal Private Tour
description:
Join Susan for a captivating 90-minute walking tour along a lesser-known stretch of Regent’s Canal, from Islington to Hackney. This fascinating journey unveils the canal’s transformation from an industrial waterway lined with slum housing to a vibrant and creative hub.

Wander along tranquil towpaths and discover how the canal has been revitalised with houseboats, floating gardens, and eye-catching street art. Learn about the canal’s industrial past and its role in shaping London’s urban landscape, while exploring its modern-day appeal as a thriving cultural hotspot.

Along the way, hear stories of legendary entertainers such as Marie Lloyd and Alfred Hitchcock, who once lived and worked nearby. As you stroll thr

A vibrant finish at Broadway Market, where you can explore independent shops, cafés, and a bustling street food scene.

name: Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde: a Private Tour
description:
Step into the shadows of Gothic London

Step into the gas-lit streets of Victorian London and uncover the eerie tale of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. Set in the West End, particularly Soho, Robert Louis Stevenson’s gothic novella explores the duality of human nature—respectable society on one side, shadowy backstreets on the other.

This walking tour takes you through locations that mirror the novel’s descriptions, bringing London’s darker past to life. Led by a London tour guide Hazel, you'll gain insight into Stevenson’s inspirations, the contrasts of 19th-century London, and the anxieties that shaped his chilling story.

London in 1886 was a city of extremes—rapidly industrialising, filled with gas-lit alleyways, and home to both extreme wealth and desperate poverty. These stark contrasts added to the eerie atmosphere of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde, making London itself feel like a character in the novel.

This walk is perfect for literature lovers, GCSE students, and those intrigued by the mysteries of London’s past**. Step into Stevenson’s world and experience one of literature’s most haunting tales.

What Can Customers Expect?
highlights: A captivating and immersive tour led by an experienced London guide.
The chance to walk through the very streets that inspired the eerie setting of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde.
Live readings of key excerpts from the novel to bring the story to life.
A deeper understanding of Victorian London—from its opulence to its dark underbelly.
Fascinating insights into Robert Louis Stevenson’s life and inspirations.
A friendly, engaging atmosphere where questions and discussions are encouraged.
Expert Tour Guide – Led by a qualified and knowledgeable London tour guide.
Small Group Experience - Enjoy a more personal and interactive walking tour.

name: Scientific Secrets and Literary Legends Tour
description:
Overview:
Explore the remarkable connections between science and literature in 18th and 19th century London on this private walking tour through Mayfair, Soho, and St James’s. Discover how scientific breakthroughs inspired iconic literary creations, from Frankenstein to Dracula. Ideal for GCSE, A-Level students, or curious minds keen to explore where fact and fiction meet.

Step back into the age of discovery and explore the stories behind some of Britain’s greatest scientific thinkers and the writers they inspired. From Newton’s gravity to Frankenstein’s monster, this tour brings history vividly to life through captivating storytelling and fascinating facts.

Led by a qualified London guide, this tour explores how breakthroughs in chemistry, anatomy, computing and more shaped some of our most memorable literary figures. You’ll learn how Mary Shelley was influenced by galvanism, how Ada Lovelace helped envision modern computing, and how gothic horror emerged from real-life experiments and intellectual debates.

Perfect for literature lovers, science buffs and students alike, this tour offers a
highlights: An engaging 90-minute private tour through the heart of historic Mayfair, Soho and St James’s, with stories that connect science, literature and the people behind both.

The chance to learn about groundbreaking scientific achievements and how they sparked the imaginations of writers like Mary Shelley, Bram Stoker, and Robert Louis Stevenson.
A relaxed and informative experience, suitable for older children, students and adults with an interest in literature, science or London’s cultural history.
A unique opportunity to visit key locations tied to some of Britain’s most influential minds, both real and fictional.

name: Private Medieval London Tour
description:
Step back in time to medieval London, a city of thriving trade, powerful guilds, and deep religious devotion—but also of filth, disease, and danger. This guided walk will transport you to a time when merchants, craftsmen, and pilgrims filled the bustling streets, while open sewers and plague outbreaks were a daily reality.

What Can Customers Expect?

Engaging Storytelling – Our expert guide will bring medieval London to life with vivid tales of its people, trade, and customs.
Untold History – Explore streets whose names—like Bread Street and Ironmonger Lane—still reflect their medieval origins.
Medieval Survival Guide – Learn what it took to live, work, and thrive in London’s most challenging era.
The Good, the Bad & the Smelly – From grand churches to open sewers, experience the full spectrum of medieval life.
A Journey Through Time – Walk the very streets where London’s merchants, monks, and miscreants once roamed.

description:
Enjoy a relaxing and scenic punting tour along the gentle waters of the River Cherwell. As you glide south from the boathouse, your chauffeur will ease the punt along the riverbank, giving you uninterrupted views of Oxford’s beautiful Botanic Gardens — the oldest in the UK — with their iconic glasshouses and carefully tended plant collections. Continuing downstream, you’ll drift past the playing fields of Magdalen College School and then enter the peaceful expanse of Christ Church Meadows.

name: British Museum Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Discover the British Museum, home to one of the world’s most extraordinary collections of over eight million artifacts spanning six millennia! On this small-group tour—limited to just eight guests—our expert guide will transport you across continents and through time, revealing the remarkable stories behind the museum’s most treasured exhibits.

Unravel the mysteries of Ancient Egypt before the Rosetta Stone and the Mummy of Katebet. Marvel at the Parthenon Marbles, masterpieces of Greek classical sculpture that once adorned the Acropolis. Next, journey east to the empires of Assyria and Babylon where you’ll encounter the colossal winged bulls that once guarded the palace of ancient kings.

The Rosetta Stone
The key to deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphs, this artifact unlocked the secrets of an ancient civilization and remains one of the museum’s most famous treasures.

The Parthenon Marbles

The Egyptian Mummies
Explore the museum’s world-class collection of mummies, sarcophagi, and burial artifacts, offering a glimpse into ancient Egyptian beliefs about the afterlife.

name: National Gallery of London Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Discover the masterpieces of the National Gallery on this 2.5-hour small-group tour, limited to just eight guests. Home to one of the world’s finest collections of European painting, the gallery spans centuries of artistic innovation, from the Renaissance to Impressionism. Led by an expert guide, this tour unveils the techniques, stories, and historical impact behind some of the most celebrated works of art, bringing both the paintings and their creators to life.

Trace the evolution of painting from the Renaissance masters—Leonardo, Raphael, and Michelangelo—to the drama of the Baroque, where Caravaggio’s The Supper at Emmaus stuns with its dramatic use of light and shadow. Compare the meticulous precision of Vermeer and Van Eyck before moving into the modern age, where Turner’s seascapes, Monet and Seurat’s shimmering Impressionist visions, and Van Gogh’s Sunflowers changed the course of art forever.

By the end of

Leonardo da Vinci’s The Virgin of the Rocks
Marvel at this masterful composition blending ethereal light, intricate details, and mysterious symbolism.

Velázquez’s The Rokeby Venus
Admire the beauty and controversy of this sensuous depiction of the goddess of love, once slashed by a suffragette in protest.

name: History of London - City Center Westminster Guided Walking Tour – Semi-Private
description:
Discover London’s royal and political heart on this 2.5-hour small-group walking tour through Westminster. From the grandeur of Buckingham Palace to the historic halls of Parliament, uncover the stories of monarchs, prime ministers, and revolutionaries as you walk through London’s most iconic squares and landmarks.

Explore the city’s most famous sites, from the bustling Covent Garden to Trafalgar Square, named after Britain’s greatest naval victory and home to Nelson’s Column and the National Gallery. Follow the historic route through Whitehall, passing Britain’s most important government buildings, and try to spot the Prime Minister outside 10 Downing Street, their official residence for over 300 years. Discuss World War II outside the Churchill War Rooms, and examine the turbulent relationship between the monarchy and Parliament as you pass royal palaces. name: Old City of London Guided Walking Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Step back in time on this 2.5-hour small-group walking tour through the Old City of London, where medieval streets, ancient ruins, and grand landmarks reveal the city’s rich and turbulent past. Once the heart of Roman Londinium, later the epicenter of royal power, financial might, and devastating fires, this historic district holds the secrets of London’s evolution from a walled Roman settlement to a modern metropolis.

Walk in the footsteps of knights, kings, and rebels as you explore Guildhall, the site of London’s medieval government, and uncover the hidden Roman amphitheater buried beneath the city. Marvel at the grandeur of St Paul’s Cathedral, where King Charles and Princess Diana wed in 1981, and learn about the Great Fire of 1666, which reshaped the city’s skyline. Admire the legendary London Bridge, a crossing that has stood for over 2,000 years, before reaching the iconic Tower Bridge and the imposing Tower of London, home to the Crown

St Paul’s Cathedral (EXTERIOR ONLY)
One of London’s most iconic landmarks, St Paul’s Cathedral has stood as a symbol of resilience since its reconstruction after the Great Fire of 1666.

Famous for its Bow Bells, this church is deeply woven into Cockney culture—legend has it that only those born within earshot of its bells can claim to be true Cockneys.

Designed by Christopher Wren, this towering column marks the site where the Great Fire of 1666 began, destroying most of the city.

London Bridge & Tower Bridge

name: East End (Street Art) Guided Walking Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Brush up on your Cockney slang, rub shoulders with the Shoreditch hipsters, and soak up the buzz of Brick Lane on this small-group walking tour of London’s East End, where centuries-old markets, immigrant communities, and constantly evolving street art come together to define one of the city’s most exciting districts.

Discover the Victorian era Spitalfields Market and walk the cobbled streets of Whitechapel, once home to London’s working class and the hunting ground of Jack the Ripper. Along the way, take in the striking murals and graffiti that make the East End a global hotspot for street art. Stroll along Brick Lane, famous for its markets, curry houses, and vibrant art scene, before making your way to Shoreditch, where trendy bars, vintage shops, and former industrial spaces have been transformed into some of the city’s most innovative creative hubs.

This tour offers a captivating blend of history and modern culture, from historic marketplace

name: London SoHo District Guided Walking Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Explore the vibrant streets of Soho, London’s historic entertainment district, on this small-group walking tour. Known for its theatres, music venues, and countercultural movements, Soho has been the epicenter of fashion, rebellion, and creativity for centuries. From Piccadilly Circus’s bright lights to the historic alleyways where rock legends, literary greats, and radical thinkers left their mark, uncover the stories that shaped this legendary neighborhood.

Stroll through Carnaby Street, the heart of Swinging Sixties fashion, where designers like Mary Quant and Vivienne Westwood transformed British style. Pause at the Broad Street Pump, where Dr. John Snow’s groundbreaking discovery changed public health forever. Walk along Old Compton Street, home to London’s LGBTQ+ community, and hear the story of the Admiral Duncan pub, a landmark of resilience. name: Natural History Museum of London Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Step into the Natural History Museum, one of London’s most awe-inspiring institutions, where the wonders of the natural world come to life. From towering dinosaur skeletons to rare gemstones, this small-group tour—limited to just eight guests—explores the museum’s most remarkable treasures.

Housed in a grand Romanesque building, the museum itself is a masterpiece, its cathedral-like architecture reflecting the grandeur of nature. In the vast Hintze Hall, gaze up at Hope, the 25-meter-long blue whale skeleton suspended from the ceiling. Then, in the Earth Hall, come face to face with Sophie the Stegosaurus, one of the most complete Stegosaurus skeletons ever discovered. Discover Cheddar Man, Britain’s oldest known human, before beholding one of the most significant books in scientific history—a first edition of Darwin’s On the Origin of Species, which revolutionized our understanding of evolution.

Led by an expert guide, this tour offers a fascin

One of the most significant fossils ever found, this 150-million-year-old specimen provides crucial evidence of the evolutionary link between dinosaurs and birds.

First Edition of On the Origin of Species
A cornerstone of scientific thought, this 1859 first edition of On the Origin of Species is preserved in the museum’s Treasures Gallery.

name: Private Euston to Kings Cross London Tour
description:
Discover the fascinating contrasts of Euston and King’s Cross on this walking tour, where history meets modern redevelopment. Once known for its industrial past, this area is now a dynamic hub of culture, architecture, and transformation.

Starting at Euston Station, we’ll explore lesser-known Georgian streets, elegant crescents, and iconic landmarks, including:
A grand railway hotel with a fascinating past.

The world-famous library that has shaped literary culture.
The architectural masterpiece of King’s Cross Station.
Follow in the footsteps of Charles Dickens and Poet Laureate John Betjeman, uncovering the stories that connect them to this ever-changing part of London.
highlights: Explore a district in flux – See how Euston and King’s Cross have evolved from industrial and transport hubs into thriving centres of culture and innovation.
Walk through time – Stroll along historic Georgian crescents, secret alleyways, and grand railway stations, uncovering their fascinating past.
Uncover literary connections – Follow in the footsteps of Charles Dickens and Poet Laureate John Betjeman, and discover the stories that link them to this dynamic area.
Discover architectural gems – Marvel at the stunning transformation of King’s Cross Station and the grandeur of St Pancras, alongside lesser-known historical sites.
Engaging storytelling – Led by a qualified guide, the walk brings history to life with insightful commentary and captivating anecdotes.

name: Private Historic Pub Tour: Covent Garden
description:
Step into London’s pub history with a private tour through the charming streets and tucked-away taverns of Covent Garden. With over 3,000 pubs across the city, we’ve handpicked six of the most historic and characterful establishments — each one with its own story to tell.

This relaxed and engaging walk explores more than just local landmarks; it brings to life centuries of tradition, rebellion, and revelry. From the shadows of the old fruit and veg market to the alleys once roamed by actors and anarchists, your professional guide will share stories that are as rich as the ales on offer.

This is no rowdy pub crawl — it’s a social stroll through time, with plenty of opportunities to sip (or skip) drinks and enjoy Covent Garden’s warm pub hospitality. Non-drinkers are more than welcome, and every pub offers a variety of non-alcoholic refreshments.

Ideal fo
highlights: A private and personable walking tour exploring six of Covent Garden’s most historic and atmospheric pubs.

Opportunities to step inside a variety of historic pubs, each with its own charm, character, and architectural quirks.
A relaxed and sociable atmosphere — this is not a pub crawl, but a cultural stroll with optional drinks at each stop.

name: Half Day London Highlights Tour
description:

This 4-hour private walking tour is the perfect introduction to London for first-time visitors. Explore iconic landmarks, hear fascinating stories of London’s history, and experience the city like a local. From the majestic Big Ben to the royal splendor of Buckingham Palace, this tour takes you through the heart of London, with a few hidden gems along the way.

Your expert guide will not only show you the sights but will also share insider knowledge on everything from royal history to how to navigate the London Underground. Whether you’re here for a few days or just a few hours, this tour will give you a memorable and comprehensive introduction to the city.

name: Small Group Intimate Churchill's London & War Rooms Museum Tour
description:
Winston Churchill plotted our victory from the streets of London
On this deeply personal tour, we will visit the lesser-known streets, shops and buildings which played a huge role in Churchill's life and self-image. You'll check out the shop where he purchased his iconic cigars, stand in the military parade ground with its own hidden WW2 communications centre, visit the wine merchant with cellars running beneath the street, and take in the social clubs of which he was a member.

Hear about his relationships to his wife Clementine and President Roosevelt, his life as a young soldier, and the stories of valour and predestiny which shaped his journey to become the man of the hour.

And then for the mind blowing trip into the Churchill War Rooms. Here you'll be able to self-guide through London's most atmospheric and haunting museum, literally entering a time capsule from London in the War.

What's included

Visit the cigar shop frequented by Winston Churchill

name: Royal Tour w/ Buckingham Palace Entry! Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Immerse yourself in London’s royal heritage on this 2-hour small group walking tour (limited to just 12 guests), tracing the history of the monarchy’s through grand parks, historic palaces, and iconic monuments. From Hyde Park, once King Henry VIII’s hunting ground, to the gates of Buckingham Palace, uncover the evolving relationship between the monarchy and Parliament before culminating with timed entry to Buckingham Palace for a self-guided visit.

Gain modern insights into the Royal Family, including their portrayal in The Crown, as you take in panoramic views of Buckingham Palace from Constitution Hill. Hear gripping accounts of assassination attempts, palace break-ins, and the English Civil War as you pass Clarence House and the Tudor-era St. James’s Palace, long-standing symbols of royal power. The tour continues through trendy Borough Yards to a popular donut destination known for inventive flavors. The experience concludes near Tower Bridge, with handcrafted donuts from Crosstown Doughnuts, offering iconic riverside views and a sweet taste of London history. 🍩🇬🇧

description:
Your Brighton donut adventure begins at Glazed – Next Level Donuts and Coffee, a local favorite known for bold, creative donuts that push the boundaries of classic flavors. As you sample these treats, you’ll learn about Brighton’s evolution from a small fishing village into a vibrant seaside destination. Next, you’ll stop at a cozy café serving delicious vegan donuts, reflecting the city’s inclusive, sustainability-focused culture. Along the way, your guide will share stories of Brighton’s thriving arts scene, iconic festivals, and free-spirited reputation. The tour continues with a relaxing tea stop paired with tales from the Regency era. The experience wraps up at the iconic Brighton Palace Pier, where fresh donuts, sea air, and history create a sweet seaside finale.

name: New Forest Food Safari with Brunch
description:
Hop on board your retro style e-bike for a full day of power assisted biking and brunching along the picturesque Solent coastline wending your way through the centuries old New Forest country lanes and woodland trails. Five levels of cheeky pedal assist means all the fun without any of the sweat!

Stop along the way for a well-earned leisurely brunch and beat the rush! Busy or not, on arrival you have a guaranteed VIP table for a tasty Full English Breakfast and bottomless tea and coffee in the pretty New Forest village of Sway.

Your head turning e-bikes have chunky fat tyres with five levels of cheeky pedal assist at your fingertips, meaning there's no need to avoid those pesky hills or limit your journey or imagination. The bikes comes in two frame types and with a nippy little 250w battery it puts everyone on an equal footing so that nobody gets left behind!

🚴‍♂️ Unforgettable Adventures Await!

name: Oxford University & City Walking Tour
description:
Explore Oxford University Colleges & The Dreaming Spires

Award-winning 2-hour tour! Over 1,400 years of history awaits within the city, from the Saxons to the present day, our expert guides will walk you through the stories of Oxford’s most famous colleges and landmarks on this award winning 2 hour tour.

From the Radcliffe Camera to the oldest colleges, via the historic Covered Market, our friendly and knowledgeable guides will escort you through the hidden alleyways, regaling you with tales of Oxford’s famous and infamous.

With famous Alumni from authors to inventors and Prime Ministers, Oxford’s rich history is delivered by our guides in their own personal style. Every tour is unique, so book again to have a whole new experience!

What's included

name: Liverpool with a Local: Walking Tour
description:
Get your bearings and discover Liverpool through a Scouser’s eyes on our daily tour with a local, expert guide. Immerse yourself in the sights and sounds of the city as you travel back through the centuries to discover our turbulent history, and how it has shaped Liverpool into the vibrant city of culture you see today.

Over 1.5 hours, you’ll not only uncover Liverpool’s past but also get an authentic glimpse into what it means to be a Liverpudlian today. Along the way, explore hidden gems and iconic landmarks, from the legendary docks and the city’s Titanic connections to the famous mythical Liver Birds and, of course, The Beatles! Whether you're a history buff, music lover, or just curious about the city's soul, there's something for everyone.

The tour includes:
– Pier Head & The Waterfront
– The Albert Docks
– Liver Building
– Castle Street
– Liverpool Town Hall
– The Three Graces
– The Cavern Club
– Tate Liverpool
And many hidden treasures!

Highlights
Explore Liverpool’s waterfront and Albert Docks
Discover Titanic connections and the city’s turbulent history
See iconic sites including the Liver Building and the Cavern Club
Experience local stories and Scouse perspective on the city
Visit cultural venues such as Tate Liverpool

name: Combo: British Museum + National Gallery of London Guided Tour - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Experience the very best of art and history on our 5.5 hour semi-private guided tour of two of London’s most renowned institutions: the British Museum and the National Gallery. From ancient civilizations to Renaissance masterpieces, this tour offers an expertly curated selection of highlights, ensuring an engaging and immersive experience.

Begin at the British Museum, home to some of the world’s most extraordinary artifacts. Unravel the mysteries of Ancient Egypt before the Rosetta Stone, the key to deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphs. Marvel at the Parthenon Marbles, sculptural masterpieces from Athens’ most famous temple. Return to Britain and trace its history from Roman rule to the medieval era, uncovering the intricate treasures of the Sutton Hoo burial and exquisite Roman silverware. Along the way, uncover stories of empires, lost civilizations, and the objects that shaped human history.

After a short break, head to the National Gallery a
highlights: Semi-Private Combo Museum Tour (with Reserved Entry Tickets included)
"Semi-Private" means group size is never more than 8 guests maximum
Professional Tour guide

Rosetta Stone
The key to deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphs, this artifact unlocked the mysteries of an ancient civilization, making it one of the most important discoveries in linguistic history.

Parthenon Marbles

Lewis Chessmen
A striking set of 12th-century Viking-era chess pieces, carved from walrus ivory, offering a glimpse into medieval craftsmanship and Norse influence in the British Isles.

name: London SoHo District Guided Walking Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Explore the vibrant streets of Soho, London’s historic entertainment district, on this private walking tour. Known for its theatres, music venues, and countercultural movements, Soho has been the epicenter of fashion, rebellion, and creativity for centuries. From Piccadilly Circus’s bright lights to the historic alleyways where rock legends, literary greats, and radical thinkers left their mark, uncover the stories that shaped this legendary neighborhood.

Stroll through Carnaby Street, the heart of Swinging Sixties fashion, where designers like Mary Quant and Vivienne Westwood transformed British style. Pause at the Broad Street Pump, where Dr. John Snow’s groundbreaking discovery changed public health forever. Walk along Old Compton Street, home to London’s LGBTQ+ community, and hear the story of the Admiral Duncan pub, a landmark of resilience. Discuss the district's nobility at Soho Square, before reaching Chinatown, where authentic restaurants and cu
highlights:
Golden Square
Step into Golden Square, a hidden gem with a rich aristocratic past, now home to some of London’s most influential media and creative businesses.

London Palladium
Pass by the legendary London Palladium, one of Britain’s most famous theatres, where Charlie Chaplin, The Beatles, and modern-day stars have graced the stage.

Carnaby Street
Wander through Carnaby Street, the beating heart of Swinging Sixties fashion, where designers like Mary Quant and Vivienne Westwood revolutionized British style.

The Broad Street Pump
Visit the Broad Street Pump, where Dr. John Snow’s discovery of contaminated water changed the course of public health history.

Berwick Street Market
Explore Berwick Street Market, a vibrant street market dating back over 200 years, known for its independent traders, record shops, and food stalls.

Meard Street
Stroll down Meard Street, a charming Georgian-era street that has long been a haven for artists, activists, and writers.

Dean Street
Walk along Dean Street, once frequented by Karl Marx, Oscar Wilde, and The Rolling Stones, and hear the stories of Soho’s rebellious past.

Soho Square
Take in the tranquil greenery of Soho Square, surrounded by historic buildings and the creative studios that keep Soho’s bohemian spirit alive.

name: Natural History Museum of London Guided Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Step into the Natural History Museum, one of London’s most awe-inspiring institutions, where the wonders of the natural world come to life. From towering dinosaur skeletons to rare gemstones, this private tour explores the museum’s most remarkable treasures.

Housed in a grand Romanesque building, the museum itself is a masterpiece, its cathedral-like architecture reflecting the grandeur of nature. In the vast Hintze Hall, gaze up at Hope, the 25-meter-long blue whale skeleton suspended from the ceiling. Then, in the Earth Hall, come face to face with Sophie the Stegosaurus, one of the most complete Stegosaurus skeletons ever discovered. Discover Cheddar Man, Britain’s oldest known human, before beholding one of the most significant books in scientific history—a first edition of Darwin’s On the Origin of Species, which revolutionized our understanding of evolution.

Led by an expert guide, this tour offers a fascinating journey through time, from prehistoric giants t
highlights:
Hope the Blue Whale
Marvel at Hope, the 25.2-meter-long blue whale skeleton, suspended majestically from the ceiling of Hintze Hall.

Archaeopteryx Fossil
One of the most significant fossils ever found, this 150-million-year-old specimen provides crucial evidence of the evolutionary link between dinosaurs and birds.

First Edition of On the Origin of Species
A cornerstone of scientific thought, this 1859 first edition of On the Origin of Species is preserved in the museum’s Treasures Gallery.

"An amazing way to see the coast of North Devon. It was so exhilarating and so much fun. Thank you so much for making it one of the best experiences I’ve ever had."

Why Coasteer With Surfing Croyde Bay?

Join us for an unforgettable coasteering experience at Baggy Point, Croyde, guided by our expert team.

We’ll take you safely across North Devon’s rugged coastline, where you’ll jump from rocky ledges, weave through narrow gullies, and discover hidden caves tucked beneath the cliffs. With routes designed for different skill levels, there’s an adventure suited for everyone.

name: Axe Throwing
description:
This exciting 1-hour activity is perfect for parties and group events, offering a chance to learn the thrilling art of axe throwing. Inspired by ancient warriors—from medieval knights and Vikings to Native American tribes skilled in tomahawk throwing—this experience brings history to life in a fun and engaging way.

The session kicks off with an introduction to the fundamentals of safely and accurately throwing lightweight axes at wooden roundel targets from a distance of 3 meters. Everyone will get a feel for the technique before moving on to larger, more challenging axes, which offer a deeply satisfying throw.

To wrap up, the group will compete in a friendly, scored competition using tournament target boards.

What's included

description:
See Birmingham through a true Brummie’s eyes on this 1.5-hour walking tour. Immerse yourself in the city's sights and sounds as you journey through centuries of our history, uncovering the truth of the city of a thousand trades.

During the tour, you’ll discover roots of Norman nobility to titans of the Industrial Revolution, charting Birmingham's path as a city of innovation to its place today as the UK’s second-largest city. See key historic sights like Birmingham Cathedral, Chamberlain Square and the canals, while uncovering the stories and people behind them. You’ll also gain real insight into life in Birmingham today, with every guide bringing their own stories of the city.

name: Changing the Guard
description:
Rather than standing still for hours, we see various stages of the ceremony, including the inspection, Old Guard, New Guard, Palaces, and even march alongside the Guards and Ceremonial Bands! Our tour guides have the experience, knowledge and humour to make sure you leave with an enviable set of pictures, an insight into what is going on, an anecdote or two, and most importantly, a memory which will last forever. We are endorsed by www.changing-guard.com and so have the backing of the most knowledgeable people out there.

Sadly, a great number of people go to see this world famous ceremony and leave disappointed. They stand at the gates of Buckingham Palace and wait uncomfortably for the action to happen, but are unaware that they have missed the majority of the pomp, ceremony, music and marching that takes place elsewhere. And that is why a Fun London Guide is invaluable in getting you around. If you like a fast paced tour, with plenty of action, excitement and fun, come on this walk. In order to stay ahead of the crowds we do have to move quickly at times - at one point we will even be marching alongside the band! We will also encounter at least one flight of steps so unfortunately it is not suitable for people with walking difficulties.

Please Note: We will not stan

name: Private Greenwich Walking Tour
description:
Come and see some of the finest and most important buildings in England.

Greenwich was for centuries the ceremonial entrance to the capital from the river. It was the site of one the most important Tudor palaces, and later of the Royal Naval Hospital where a collection of the greatest names in English architecture worked, including Wren and Hawksmoor.

In the middle of the hospital is the Queen’s House, which introduced Italian renaissance architecture to England, and in the park is the Royal Observatory, where Charles II’s Astronomer Royal John Flamsteed mapped the night sky.

The presence of the palace, and then the sailors ensured a rich and various history for the town. Explore Greenwich’s magisterial and its seedy sides, and hear about its literary connections on this fascinating walking tour.

description:
Join your characterful witch guide and hear the true historical tales of suspected Yorkshire witches across the ages. Your friendly costumed guide will enchant you on this exciting escapade, seamlessly weaving history with vibrant storytelling.

But did these Yorkshire 'witches' really deserve their fates? Cross York’s cobbles and uncover the truth about the likes of Mad Alice - was she driven to insanity? How about John Wrightson, was he really a "Wrong-son"? Mary Bateman; wise woman or simply a murderer? And did Margaret Clitheroe really deserve her terrible ending…?

Delve into some fascinating York history along the way too as you're transported through the twists and turns of York's gates (streets!) and snickelways. You’ll shuffle down Shambles, sidle through King's Square, amble along “Bookbinders Alley” and saunter down Stonega

name: London: Witches & History Bankside Walking Tour
description:
Join your characterful witch guide and hear the true historical tales of suspected London witches across the ages. Witness fascinating pieces of London history along the way too; secrets of Southwark Cathedral, The Golden Hinde, The Globe Theatre, Millennium Bridge and so very much more await on this character led walking tour.

Starting by the infamous London Bridge your friendly costumed witch guide will lead you across Southwark's Southbank’s cobbles, seamlessly weaving history with vibrant storytelling. Widen your eyes to the tales of London’s accused witches as you uncover the truth about the likes of Nicholas Culpepper – people’s herbalist or bad boy herb doctor? Was Elizabeth Barton really that gifted? How about Jinny Bingham - did she deserve the nickname “Mother Damnable"? And did Old Joan Butts get off Scot-Free? You make your own mind up on this enlightening educational journey to the past.

You will wind your way down Winchester Walk, bob around the edges of Borough Market

name: Suffragettes of Westminster Tour
description:
Walk in the footsteps of the fearless women who fought for equality and transformed British history. This walking tour takes you through the heart of Westminster, where the Suffragette movement made its mark. Discover the key figures, bold protests, and historic locations that shaped the fight for women’s suffrage.

Step into the world of the Suffragettes and learn about the fearless women who defied societal norms to demand change. This tour takes you through Westminster, where their struggle unfolded in powerful and dramatic ways.

What Can Customers Expect?

A thought-provoking journey through the streets of Westminster, uncovering the courage and resilience of the Suffragettes.
Insights into key figures such as Millicent Fawcett, Emmeline Pankhurst, and her daughters Christabel and Sylvia, as well as their differing approaches to activism.
Firsthand accounts of the daring protests, arrests, and hunger strikes that defined the movement.
Visits to historic sites, including the Suffragette Memorial and locations of major protests such as Bla
highlights: A thought-provoking journey through the streets of Westminster, uncovering the courage and resilience of the Suffragettes.
Insights into key figures such as Millicent Fawcett, Emmeline Pankhurst, and her daughters Christabel and Sylvia, as well as their differing approaches to activism.
Firsthand accounts of the daring protests, arrests, and hunger strikes that defined the movement.
Visits to historic sites, including the Suffragette Memorial and locations of major protests such as Black Friday 1910.
A 90-minute immersive experience with a knowledgeable guide who brings the history of women’s suffrage to life.
A chance to reflect on the Suffragettes’ legacy and how their fight for equality continues to shape society today.

York has a rich history, from mediaeval times right up to today, this tour will bring the city's rich past to life as you stroll through our iconic cobbled streets, ancient walls, and hidden corners.

On our tour, step back in time to Viking-age Jorvik, walk in the footsteps of Romans along the city’s famous walls, and uncover how York evolved from a medieval religious centre to the vibrant, historic gem it is today. name: Rapid Kayak Safari
description:
A fun-packed 5km river journey that dives straight into action, starting with splashy Grade 1–2 rapids before easing into a relaxed, scenic paddle downstream.

You’ll hit the exciting white water early on, giving you an instant taste of river adventure, then settle into a sociable paddle through Matlock Bath, with time to enjoy the surroundings, chat, and build confidence as the river mellows. The trip finishes at the iconic Cromford Mills, rounding off the experience in a beautiful, historic setting.

Ideal for families, couples, holiday-makers, and groups, this kayak safari is great for first-timers while still being engaging for paddlers with experience. You’ll paddle our stable, responsive NRS Outlaw kayaks and be guided throughout by an experienced river guide, helping you read the water, navigate features, and make the most of every section.

name: Intro To Paddleboarding
description:
Our Intro to SUP session is the perfect starting point for anyone new to paddleboarding, or for those wanting to build confidence before heading out on a longer journey.

Set on a calm, sheltered section of the River Derwent, this session focuses on the fundamentals of stand up paddleboarding in a relaxed, supportive environment. You’ll learn how to get started safely, find your balance, paddle efficiently, and steer your board with confidence.

Led by experienced Rapid Horizons instructors, the session includes plenty of time on the water to practise at your own pace. There’s no pressure to stand straight away — you can begin kneeling and progress as your confidence grows. Coaching is tailored to the group, with tips and encouragement throughout.

This session is ideal preparation for our SUP Safari, or simply a great standalone experience if you’re looking for a

name: Churchill War Rooms and Wartime London Walk - Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Embark on an unforgettable journey through Churchill’s London and the Churchill War Rooms on this 2.5-hour small group (limited to just 8 guests) guided tour. Discover the life and achievements of Britain’s most celebrated wartime leader as you stroll along historic Whitehall, a hub of political and military activity during World War II and home to monuments such as the Whitehall Cenotaph, a poignant memorial to those who served and fell in battle.

Deepen your knowledge of the World Wars at the statue of Douglas Haig, and don’t miss the opportunity to take an iconic photograph at Horse Guards Parade before arriving at the highlight of your tour: the Churchill War Rooms. Step back in time as our guide leads you through this preserved wartime bunker. From the day the doors were locked at the end of the war to today, nothing has changed. description:
Embark on an unforgettable journey through Churchill’s London and the Churchill War Rooms on this 2.5-hour private tour. Discover the life and achievements of Britain’s most celebrated wartime leader as you stroll along historic Whitehall, a hub of political and military activity during World War II and home to monuments such as the Whitehall Cenotaph, a poignant memorial to those who served and fell in battle.

Deepen your knowledge of the World Wars at the statue of Douglas Haig, and don’t miss the opportunity to take an iconic photograph at Horse Guards Parade before arriving at the highlight of your tour: the Churchill War Rooms. Step back in time as our guide leads you through this preserved wartime bunker. From the day the doors were locked at the end of the war to today, nothing has changed. Bear witness to one of history’s most dramatic episodes, frozen in time. Explore the map room, Churchill’s office, his notebooks, and even one of his cigars. A

name: 剑桥校友伴你行 - 剑桥大学步行导览 & 康河撑篙游船之旅（私人小团）
description:
通过这项结合 步行导览与康河撑篙体验 的行程，从多个角度探索剑桥，全面了解这座城市的历史、学院与传统。

行程首先由剑桥大学学子或具有学术背景的专业导游带领进行步行导览。沿着剑桥历史悠久的街道前行时，您的导游将介绍剑桥大学独特的学院制度，并分享学生生活、著名校友、学院竞争以及延续 800 多年的校园传统。

在导览途中，您将经过剑桥最著名的学院，包括 国王学院、三一学院和圣约翰学院，了解这所大学如何发展成为世界最具影响力的学术中心之一。

希望进一步丰富体验的客人可以选择 升级进入国王学院礼拜堂（视当天开放情况而定）。在导游的历史与建筑背景介绍之后，您将有机会走进这座世界最著名的哥特式礼拜堂之一。

步行导览结束后，行程将在 康河 上继续展开。客人将前往 Mill Lane 靠近 Mill Pond 的撑篙码头 Punting Station，参加由 Scudamore’s 运营的拼船撑篙体验。Scudamore’s 是剑桥历史最悠久的撑篙公司，也是 Alumni Tours 的长期合作伙伴。

经验丰富的船夫将带领您沿着著名的 The Backs 学院后园河段 前行，并现场讲解河岸两侧的学院建筑、桥梁、花园以及大学传统。

在宁静的河面上缓缓滑行，穿过历史悠久的桥梁，从全新的角度欣赏优雅的学院建筑，这是剑桥最具代表性的体验之一。

这项 步行导览与撑篙组合行程 将历史故事讲解与轻松的康河游览完美结合，非常适合希望在一次体验中全面了解剑桥的游客。

What's included

独享一位剑桥校友或学术背景专业中文讲解员

90 分钟步行导览

本地推荐与旅行建议
