name: School group: Private Wonders of Whitehall Tour
description:
This tour explores Whitehall and Westminster as the political heart of the United Kingdom, from medieval royal courts to modern democracy and global diplomacy. Students encounter iconic sites such as the Houses of Parliament, Westminster Abbey and government departments alongside lesser‑known corners rich in political storytelling.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect

A focused introduction to British government, monarchy, power and protest, ideal for History, Politics, Citizenship and International Relations groups.​
Site‑specific stories about monarchs, civil servants, spies and wartime leaders, helping students visualise decision‑making at national and imperial level.​
Discussion prompts on constitutional change, the balance of powers and how public memory is shaped through statues, memorials and film portrayals.​

Tour Reviews

Teresa (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
"Such an interesting walk and in beautiful sunshine too! It was the second walk we have been on led by Ian and yet again he was not only superbly informed, full of interesting extra anecdotes and facts but also humour which I find helps
highlights: Follow a route through Whitehall and its surrounding streets, exploring the site of the former Palace of Whitehall and the evolution from royal residence to government quarter.​
View exteriors of key institutions such as the Houses of Parliament and Westminster Abbey while your guide connects them to events from the civil war, empire, suffrage campaigns and world wars.​
Encounter statues and memorials to figures like Churchill, Mandela and others, using them to open discussions about leadership, commemoration and contested histories.​
End Location – Central Westminster/Whitehall, close to major landmarks, toilets, cafés and coach pick‑up points.​
Recommended Nearby Attractions – Churchill War Rooms, Westminster Abbey, the Supreme Court and nearby galleries or museums for deeper study of political or constitutional topics.​
Dining & Refreshments – Group‑friendly food options and green spaces in St James’s Park and along Whitehall where packed lunches can be eaten.​
Transport Options – Excellent onward transport via Westminster, Charing Cross or Embankment, plus frequent bus routes across central London.​
Share Your Experience – Students can be encouraged to map what they have seen onto their exam specifications or seminar topics, while teachers can share feedback online to help future groups.

name: School group: Private Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde Tour
description:
Step into the shadows of Gothic London

Step into the gas-lit streets of Victorian London and explore the unsettling world of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. Set in the West End, particularly Soho, Robert Louis Stevenson’s Gothic novella examines the dual nature of humanity—respectable public life contrasted with darker, more unsettling streets.

This curriculum-linked walking tour explores locations that reflect the atmosphere and themes of the novel, helping students connect the text to its real-world setting. Led by an experienced London guide, Hazel, the walk provides valuable context on Stevenson’s inspirations, Victorian society, and the social anxieties that shaped this enduring work of literature.

London in 1886 was a city of sharp contrasts: rapid industrial growth, gas-lit alleyways, great wealth alongside extreme poverty. These conditions helped create the tense and unsettling backdrop of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde, with the city itself feeling almost like a character in the story.

This walk is particularly suitable for GCSE English Literature students, school grou
highlights: An engaging, curriculum-relevant walking tour led by a knowledgeable London guide
Exploration of London streets that echo the novel’s setting and themes
Short live readings from key passages to support textual understanding
Clear explanations of Victorian social context and how it informs the novel
Insight into Robert Louis Stevenson’s life, influences, and writing
Opportunities for discussion, questions, and critical thinking
A small-group format that encourages interaction and participation
Meeting Point – Covent Garden Tube (exact details provided upon booking).

name: School group: Private Women in the City of London Tour
description:
This tour uncovers the often‑hidden stories of women who shaped the City of London, from medieval workers and nuns to investors, reformers and modern professionals. Students see how female ambition and resilience helped transform institutions that once excluded them.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A thematic walk ideal for History, Politics, Sociology, Gender Studies, Business and Citizenship.​
Case studies of women who founded hospitals, financed major institutions, challenged legal exclusion and campaigned for workplace equality.​
Discussion of power, profit and progress: how women resisted control, drove economic change and rewrote professional rules.​
Tour Reviews

Susan (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
"Who knew that so many extraordinary women were trailblazers over the centuries most of whom we are not even aware of? A thoroughly professional and entertaining walk with Jenny, who obviously enjoyed re
highlights: A thematic walk ideal for History, Politics, Sociology, Gender Studies, Business and Citizenship.​
Case studies of women who founded hospitals, financed major institutions, challenged legal exclusion and campaigned for workplace equality.​
Discussion of power, profit and progress: how women resisted control, drove economic change and rewrote professional rules.​
Meeting Point – Cannon Street Station concourse or nearby landmark, with exact details in your confirmation.​
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early for registration, group organisation and health and safety briefing.​
What to Bring– Comfortable shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing, water and optional worksheets focused on individuals, institutions and turning points.​
Accessibility – City streets include some gradients and busy junctions; with advance notice the route can usually be adapted for mobility needs.​
Visit former hospitals, religious sites, financial buildings, courts and civic spaces linked to women’s work, philanthropy, protest and professional life.​

name: School group: Private Heretics and Horrors Tour
description:
This tour explores a darker side of London’s past, focusing on religious conflict, heresy, punishment and public spectacle in the City and nearby. Students encounter sites linked to trials, executions and belief, seeing how power, fear and faith shaped the streets they walk today.

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A thematic walk ideal for RS, History, Politics and Law, covering heresy, persecution, conscience and the state.
Case studies spanning medieval to early modern periods that illustrate how religious difference was controlled, punished and sometimes commemorated.
Space to discuss concepts such as tolerance, freedom of belief, martyrdom, treason and the use of terror as social control.
Tour Reviews

highlights: A thematic walk ideal for RS, History, Politics and Law, covering heresy, persecution, conscience and the state.
Case studies spanning medieval to early modern periods that illustrate how religious difference was controlled, punished and sometimes commemorated.

name: School group: Private Street Art in Shoreditch Tour
description:
This tour explores London’s vibrant street art scene, using walls, shutters and alleys as an open‑air gallery of creativity, protest and community identity. Students examine how visual culture shapes conversations about power, belonging and the right to the city.

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A dynamic walk ideal for Art & Design, Media Studies, Sociology, Citizenship and Geography.
Direct engagement with works by a range of artists, from large‑scale murals to small interventions and stencils.
Discussion of themes including gentrification, branding, subculture, political protest and the line between vandalism and art.

name: Secret London Tour
description:
For those of you who want to dig deeper than the average tourist, and be rewarded with a photo and story that will be the envy of your friends back home, then the Secret London Tour is for you.

We will visit secret alleyways, ancient pubs, hear of body snatchers and murders, and reveal the unexpected and unseen. This is one memorable journey! We are privileged to live in a city with 2000 years of history. Some of the most influential people in the world have walked these streets and many have left a distinct footprint. On the Secret London Tour we will show you a side of London away from the usual tourist trail – we’ll take you along gas lit alleyways, past old pubs and bookshops, remnants of sprawling mansions, unusual art and secret societies.

So if you want to see another side to London, and enjoy exploring a city a few layers deeper than the average tourist, then come on The Secret London Tour!

What's included

Walking Tour

Highlights

On this unique and unusual tour we will show you the hidden parts of London that few people know about.

name: Private Smartphone Photo Tour: St Paul's
description:
Insider tips on great spots for photographing some of London's most iconic landmarks!

If you love the City of London’s architecture around the area of St Paul's cathedral and want to improve your photography skills on your smartphone, then sign up to Alastair's smartphone photo walk.

Alastair, a professional photographer will be your expert guide. He will lead you around the area getting the best views and explore the key aspects of it's architectural history and design.

During your St Paul's smartphone photo walkshop, Alastair will set photography challenges, nurture your photographic eye and help you develop a better understanding of what makes a great photo. You will receive insider tips on great spots for photographing some of London's most iconic landmarks.

At the end of the 2 hour tour you will have gained a collection of photos you will feel proud of and having gained insight as to how to improve your smartphone photography.

name: Secrets of St James's Tour
description:
A private walking tour uncovering the secrets of St James’s, an area known for its connection to Royalty and the aristocracy since the reign of King Henry VIII.

Hear about some of the scandalous events that occurred over the centuries in the exclusive area of St James’s on your private tour.

Scandal is something that intrigues everyone. The press feeds the headlines with the news on events that have typically erupted from greed, lust, or the abuse of power.

Today the media regularly reports on these that involve both a political or Royal sphere.

On your private tour you will look at some of the scandalous lives of the privileged few who lived in their opulent mansions, as well as those that socialised with the elite. They hardly set a good example to those less fortunate.

name: Windsor Town Walking Tour
description:
A town steeped in royal heritage and set along the picturesque River Thames, Windsor is as charming as it is captivating. From its deep ties to the British monarchy to its eclectic architecture and vibrant modern life, there’s something for everyone to discover in this idyllic town.

Join your local expert guide for a 1.5-hour stroll through the main sights of Windsor, walking in the footsteps of kings, queens, and everyday folk. From William the Conqueror’s claim on the town after the Battle of 1066 to Queen Elizabeth II’s long-standing residence at Windsor Castle, history comes to life around every corner. Along the way, you'll not only uncover Windsor’s fascinating past but also gain insight into what it’s like to live and work in this historic town today.

description:
Discover London one sweet bite at a time on this delicious walking tour through Borough and the riverside. The journey begins at St. John’s Bakery, near Borough Station, where artisanal donuts and traditional baked goods set the tone. Next, enjoy a tea stop at Bird & Blend Tea Co. near the historic Borough Market, a trading hub dating back to the 13th century. The tour continues through trendy Borough Yards to a popular donut destination known for inventive flavors. The experience concludes near Tower Bridge, with handcrafted donuts from Crosstown Doughnuts, offering iconic riverside views and a sweet taste of London history. 🍩🇬🇧

description:
Discover one of Oxford’s most traditional experiences on a private chauffeured punting tour along the River Cherwell.
This relaxing river journey offers a peaceful perspective of the city, passing Christ Church Meadows, historic college gardens, and some of Oxford’s most picturesque landscapes — with your punt reserved exclusively for your group.
Led by experienced chauffeurs, the tour combines gentle commentary with scenic views. Along the way, you will see:
Christ Church Meadows — linked to Lewis Carroll
Oxford Botanic Garden — Britain’s oldest botanic garden
Magdalen College School fields
St Hilda’s College gardens
The journey lasts approximately 60 minutes and returns to the Boathouse.
Ideal for families, couples, celebrations, and visitors seeking a more exclusive Oxford experience, this private punting tour offers a classic and atmospheric river journey — with flexible pacing and space to relax. With due

name: School group: Private Financial London Tour
description:
Financial London Tour

Bring business and economics to life with an on-foot exploration of the City of London, where global finance has been shaped over hundreds of years. This curriculum-linked walking tour is designed for teachers bringing GCSE, A level, IB or college students studying business, economics or finance.

Explore London’s Financial Legacy

Step into the heart of the City of London, where fortunes have been made—and lost—for centuries. This guided walk takes your students through the narrow alleys and grand institutions that helped shape London into one of the world's leading financial centres, making clear links to topics such as financial markets, sources of finance, risk and globalisation.
Discover the origins of the stock market, insurance and banking, and see how these industries continue to drive the UK and global economy today, reinforcing classroom learning with real-world examples.

What Teachers and Students Can Expect

Walk through historic alleyways where 17th- and 18th-century deals laid the fou
highlights: Walk through historic alleyways where 17th- and 18th-century deals laid the foundations of modern finance, ideal for discussing enterprise, risk and the growth of financial institutions.
Learn how equities, bonds, commodities and insurance first emerged, how they are traded today, and why these markets matter for businesses, governments and consumers.
Hear the stories of financial pioneers who built empires—and those who lost everything—prompting discussion around speculation, bubbles, regulation and ethical decision-making.
Gain expert insights from a Qualified City of London Guide who is experienced in adapting explanations for different academic levels and linking content explicitly to curriculum themes.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Cannon Street Station (exact details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in. The tour departs promptly.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down key examples or case‑study ideas.
Accessibility – Not step‑free. The route includes cobbled streets, uneven surfaces, and multiple steps (both up and down). If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.

name: Changing of the Guard Guided Walking Tour – Semi-Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Experience one of Britain’s most iconic traditions on this small-group walking tour through historic Westminster. With an expert guide, uncover the history behind the Changing of the Guard Ceremony as you witness this spectacular display of pageantry and precision.

Begin in the monumental Trafalgar Square, then follow the Royal Guard as they march in full ceremonial dress, accompanied by the rhythmic beat of military drums. Admire their perfectly groomed horses and distinctiv uniforms—scarlet tunics and towering bearskin hats—as you pass through Admiralty Arch, past St. James’s Palace, the most senior royal residence in Britain, and onto the historic parade grounds. Your guide will share stories of Britain’s most revered monarchs as the New Guard replaces the Old Guard in this centuries-old tradition. Conclude your tour with views of Buckingham Palace, where the guards arrive at their final post.
highlights: Semi-Private Walking Tour
"Semi-Private" means group size is never more than 12 guests maximum
Professional Tour Guide
Duration 2.5 Hours
Tour will run, rain or shine
Tour in English

Trafalgar Square
Discover London’s most famous public square, a historic gathering place named after Britain’s greatest naval victory at the Battle of Trafalgar (1805).

Changing of the Guard Parade
Experience this centuries-old military tradition as the King’s Guards march from St. James’s Palace to Wellington Barracks, dressed in full ceremonial uniform.

name: Moors, Whitby & The Yorkshire Steam Railway
description:
Operating on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Saturdays this small-group Guided Day Trip from York provides the opportunity to explore the scenic and picturesque North York Moors National Park, enjoy more than two hours in the seaside town of Whitby and take a Heritage Train ride on the North Yorkshire Moors Railway (optional extra, paid locally on the day and no need to book ahead)! You will also experience the traditional English moorland villages of Hutton-le-Hole and Goathland (Heartbeat and Harry Potter filming location) and travel back to York through the Howardian Hills, passing by Castle Howard. In Whitby, you will be able to see Whitby Abbey (inspiration for the Dracula books), visit the Captain Cook Attractions, shop for some Whitby Jet or Gothic Fashion, sample Fish & Chips from the famous Magpie Café or just enjoy the bustling port and waterfront – the choice is yours!

What's included

Comfortable 16-seat minicoaches with aircon and Wifi

Informative and friendly local guide

Pickup from centrally located meeting point near York Minister

Lots of photo stops

Simple and secure online booking

What's not included

Optional steam train ride on the North York Moors Rai

name: Leeds City Centre Walking Tour
description:
The best way to explore Leeds? Walk its streets with a local expert! This immersive 1.5-hour tour takes you beyond the guidebooks, bringing the city’s rich history and vibrant present to life.

Step back in time as you uncover how Leeds grew from an industrial powerhouse into the cultural hub it is today. From our booming textile past to the world’s oldest city music hall and the stunning Victorian shopping arcades, you'll experience our city’s evolution firsthand. Along the way, your guide will share fascinating stories, point out hidden gems, and give you an authentic glimpse into modern life in Leeds.

The tour includes:

Corn Exchange
Town Hall
Kirkgate Market
City Varieties Music Hall
Victoria Quarter
And many hidden treasures!

Price: £12 per person

Duration: 1.5 hours

name: Old Newcastle Walking Tours
description:
Join us for a tour around the fascinating remains of Old Newcastle, including free entry to Newcastle Castle!
About

This tour will bring to life the history of Newcastle which lies hidden behind the facade of the modern city.

One of our friendly and knowledgeable guides will take you from Newcastle Castle along to Monument before stopping at St Andrews, Newcastle's oldest church, past the formidable town walls taking in Friaries, merchant houses, markets, before finishing at the imposing Black Gate - the gatehouse of Newcastle's Castle.

From here you will be free to explore the amazingly preserved remains of the Black Gate and Castle Keep for yourselves - tickets are included i
highlights: Entry to Newcastle Castle included in your ticket!

name: Eating London: Heart of 'The City' Food Tour
description:
Welcome to the only food tour in the City of London (really!).

Down river from the palaces of Westminster, you’ll find the much older City of London, which dates back to Roman times and has been the epicenter of British and European life ever since.

Taste your way through the classic London dining destinations that fed the commercial, political and literary ambitions of a growing city - the oyster house, the chophouse, the chocolate house, the tavern & beyond - taking it from the middleweight capital of a small island nation to a world-famous global metropolis.

The story of the City of London is an entire novel written in wood, fire, stone, steel, glass, merchants & magic. Read it cover to cover on this completely one-of-a-kind culinary adventure through 2000 years, serving up the good, bad and bizarre tidbits of history, all framed by fantas
highlights: Steak and ale pie

Stilton cheese
British ale
Black velvet cocktail

name: Quirky Westminster Private Tour
description:
Westminster is known for its grandeur—Big Ben, Parliament, and Buckingham Palace—but beyond the famous landmarks lies a world of peculiar history, oddities, and architectural curiosities. This 90-minute walking tour takes you on an unconventional journey through the quirky side of Westminster, revealing the strange, the unexpected, and the downright bizarre.

Starting in Covent Garden, we’ll explore secret sculptures, peculiar street art, and stories of eccentric Londoners who have shaped the city in their own unique ways. You’ll step into a secret alleyway barely wide enough to walk through, hear tales of historical pranks and mysterious monuments, and discover how seemingly ordinary locations hide remarkable secrets.

As we weave through the streets towards Trafalgar Square and Admiralty Arch, you’ll see how history has left behind its quirks—whether it’s a missing statue, an architectural mistake, or an unusual wartime relic. From an ear embedded in a wall to a hidden nose on a historic arch, this tour proves that London is full of surprises—if you know where to look.

Ending near St James’s Palace, this tour offers a fresh perspectiv
highlights: Surprising Discoveries – From a mysterious ear sculpture on a Covent Garden wall to a hidden ‘nose’ at Admiralty Arch, you’ll uncover peculiar details hiding in plain sight.
Engaging Storytelling – Your expert guide will bring history to life with fascinating anecdotes, humorous tales, and little-known facts.
Plenty of Walking – This is a 90-minute walking tour, covering approximately 1.5 miles (2.4 km) with stops along the way. Expect to be on your feet for most of the experience.
Photo Opportunities – Capture the unique and unusual sights, from London’s narrowest alleyway to historic pubs filled with character.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Covent Garden Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide. We start promptly.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.
Accessibility – Not Step-free. The route involves some cobbled streets and uneven surfaces are part of the route. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.

name: Bottomless Not Brunch
description:
We’ve been making and shaking great cocktails at the Clubhouse for 5 years and we love it!

Over the past few years we’ve had some great feedback on how good our drinks menu is and we thought a great way for more people to sample our fantastic drinks offering was to jump on the Bottomless Brunch bandwagon and let you get your fill; 90 minutes of unlimited cocktails!

We had a look around to see what everyone else offers for Bottomless Brunch and we were kind of disappointed with what we found; Woo Woos, Tequila Sunrise, Sex on the Beach; sure they’re all classics and in the right place they can taste great but really they’re just jumped up spirit mixers masquerading as cocktails.

You will enjoy table service in our over 18’s only cocktail lounge area; all hail the Disco Ball

What's included

We offer a selection of 10 exclusive Clubhouse cocktails for you to choose from with something for everyone; no need to pay for upgrades, these drinks are already first class.

Order what you want, when you want; we don’t require the whole table to order the same drink.

Need a cocktail break? We also offer a range of s

name: Sea Sauna + Cold Bath | Open + Private Sessions
description:
This experience harmoniously blends tradition with modern innovation, drawing on ancient wisdom and cutting-edge wellness practices through heat therapy, cold immersion, and meaningful connection.

Unwind in the ultimate retreat: our Nordic wood-fired sea sauna and invigorating plunge bath, nestled above the stunning Croyde Bay.

Your journey begins in a tranquil coastal haven, where the sauna's soothing heat provides deep relaxation, seamlessly followed by a refreshing cold plunge to invigorate your senses and revitalise your well-being.

As you bask in the sauna's comforting warmth, you’ll be surrounded by breathtaking views of the Atlantic Ocean. The rhythmic crash of waves and the whisper of the sea breeze create a naturally calming symphony.

Embrace the contrast between the sauna's enveloping heat and the invigorating chill of the plunge tub. This unique interplay of warmth and coolness offers a serene balance, connecting you to the elements of earth, fire, and water.

What's included

Immer

The session is designed for groups and rewards coordination and quick thinking rather than prior driving experience, creating plenty of laughs and competitive moments.

name: Private Birmingham City Centre Walking Tour
description:
Take Birmingham at your own pace with your own dedicated guide on a private tour. Choose any start time, and we can pick you up at your hotel in the city centre.

See Birmingham through a true Brummie’s eyes on this 1.5-hour walking tour. Immerse yourself in the city's sights and sounds as you journey through centuries of our history, uncovering the truth of the city of a thousand trades.

During the tour, you’ll discover roots of Norman nobility to titans of the Industrial Revolution, charting Birmingham's path as a city of innovation to its place today as the UK’s second-largest city. See key historic sights like Birmingham Cathedral, Chamberlain Square and the canals, while uncovering the stories and people behind them. You’ll also gain real insight into life in Birmingham today, with every guide bringing their own stories of the city.

The tour includes:

Broad Street

And many hidden treasures!

name: Nottingham City Walking Tour
description:
Join your local Nottingham guide for a 1.5-hour walking tour that brings the city’s rich and rebellious past to life. Running daily at 10:30am, this tour doesn't just cover the history—it’s about experiencing Nottingham through the eyes of those who shaped it.

Wander through Nottingham’s oldest streets, uncovering stories of Anglo-Saxon settlers, medieval merchants, and daring outlaws. Learn how the Industrial Revolution transformed the city, explore its ancient cave system, and hear tales of Robin Hood, body snatchers, and revolutionary entrepreneurs who changed the course of history.

name: LGBT+ Westminster Private Tour
description:
Step into the heart of London’s LGBT+ history on this fascinating 90-minute walking tour through Westminster. From clandestine meeting spots to grand royal palaces, uncover the untold stories of love, defiance, and resilience that have shaped the LGBT+ experience in Britain’s capital.

As we stroll from Embankment to Parliament Square, you’ll discover:

The secret lives of monarchs, including King James I/VI and his beloved companions, reveal how attitudes towards sexuality have evolved over the centuries.

The tragic yet inspiring story of Oscar Wilde, a literary genius whose persecution highlights the struggles and triumphs of queer identity.

The extraordinary tale of the Chevalier d’Éon, a trans fencer and diplomat who challenged 18th-century gender norms.
The role of ‘molly houses’ and hidden spaces like St James’s Park, which provided sanctuary to those who dared to love in a time of criminalisation.

The sacrifices and contributions of LGBT+ individuals in wartime Britain, including Alan Turing, the war hero condemned by an intolerant society—his story leading to ‘Turing’s Law’ and the eventual recognition of past injustices.
Th
highlights: Discover the courage and resilience of individuals who defied societal norms.
Engage with fascinating storytelling that brings history to life.
Enjoy an interactive experience, with opportunities to ask questions and participate in discussions.
Leave with a deeper appreciation of LGBT+ heritage and its influence on London today.
Meeting Point– Arrive at Embankment Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).

description:
Dare to explore the dark and twisted past of London's West End? Join David Turnbull—horror writer, film enthusiast, and qualified London tour guide—for a spine-chilling journey through the haunted heart of the capital.

What Can Customers Expect?
Discover the eerie streets that inspired some of the most terrifying tales in gothic literature and horror cinema. From the dual nature of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde to the cursed soul of Dorian Gray, this tour unearths the real and fictional horrors lurking in London’s history. Learn about the origins of Penny Dreadfuls, the rise of horror in silent cinema, and how Londoners helped shape some of Hollywood’s most infamous monsters.

On this walking tour, y

name: Small Group Iconic Westminster, WW2 & Churchill War Rooms Tour
description:
How Churchill and London stood up to the Nazis
Hear about London during the Blitz, the courage of ordinary people in the face of terror, and see the city through the eyes of our iconic Prime Minister.

On this extraordinary deep dive into London's past, we take in the most iconic sights in Westminster, but seen through the light of Winston Churchill and Wartime London. Hear how Parliament coped with the heavy bombing, and how the Prime Minister played a personal role in its recovery. Discover how Westminster Abbey and Big Ben took direct hits, and Churchill himself nearly lost his life while in Downing Street.

We'll then take in the famous statue of Churchill, the often overlooked church where he married Clementine, then journey through the centre of political power - Whitehall.

After paying our respects to the cenotaph and the cavalry guard who still stand sentry, we'll finish the tour by entering the most incredible museum in London - the Churchill War Rooms. Tickets are included in the tour, although you will use an audio guide to navigate this perfectly preserved time capsule fro
highlights: Visit the extraordinary Churchill War Rooms with your guide
See Big Ben, the Houses of Parliament, Westminster Abbey, Parliament Square, Whitehall and the Household Cavalry!
Search for a hidden bomb shelter
Locate the lesser known church where Churchill married Clementine
Hear stories of bravery, courage and British stiff upper lips!
Tour in English

name: The London Notting Hill Breakfast Tour
description:
This unique London tour connects 5 authentic eateries with famous movie locations in a 2-hour small group experience.

From traditional scones and pies to innovative fusion pastries, each stop reveals how centuries of immigration have transformed British baking culture around Portobello Road Market.

Between bites, explore the neighborhood's musical and literary connections, from Bob Marley to George Orwell, and architectural beauty of the pastel streets which make every corner Instagram-worthy. Your local guide will reveal the stories behind the area's transformation from working-class enclave to bohemian haven, while ensuring you taste the best of both traditional and contemporary London baking.

name: School group: Private Fleet Street Tour
description:
This tour takes students into the heart of Britain’s press heritage, exploring Fleet Street as the historic centre of journalism and print culture. From St Paul’s to the Strand, learners encounter churches, courts, alleys and pubs that reveal how news, language and public opinion were shaped over centuries.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect

A curriculum‑friendly exploration of media history, suitable for English, History, Politics, Media Studies and Journalism groups.​

Stories of press barons, censorship, scandal and reform that illuminate concepts such as freedom of the press, propaganda and the role of the fourth estate.​
Opportunities to discuss the evolution of print technology, the professionalisation of journalism and the ethics of news‑making in a digital age.​

Tour Reviews

Lhynne (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
This was an engaging insight into the history of Fleet Street and the newspaper industry. Our guide, Jill, was ve
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at St Paul’s Tube Station (exact details provided upon booking).​
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in; the tour departs promptly to keep to your schedule.​
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing and a bottle of water; worksheets or notebooks are recommended for quotations and case‑study notes.​
Accessibility – Route includes busy pavements, some cobbled areas and uneven surfaces, with occasional steps; please contact us in advance to discuss specific access needs and possible adaptations.​
Walk from St Paul’s into Fleet Street, tracing how printing and publishing developed around the Cathedral and Stationers’ Hall.​
Visit St Bride’s, the “journalists’ church”, Gough Square (Dr Johnson’s house), Peterborough Court and historic pubs linked to press culture, stopping for short, structured explanations and Q&A.​
End Location – Outside the Old Bank of England on Fleet Street, with clear onward directions provided for teachers.​
Recommended Nearby Attractions – Royal Courts of Justice, Sir John Soane’s Museum, St Paul’s Cathedral and the London Museum sites for extended media or legal history projects.​

name: Hidden London Tour
description:
But in order to do this, we will need to explore the forgotten alleyways and medieval courtyards, look up, around & under, and peer through windows & bars.

The walk will take you into the labyrinth of tiny lanes around Fleet Street and Temple, past ancient cloisters and churches, over forgotten rivers and wells led by your guide who will share his explorer’s eye. As the local resident Samuel Johnson once said “"Why, Sir, you find no man, at all intellectual, who is willing to leave London. No, Sir, when a man is tired of London, he is tired of life; for there is in London all that life can afford." And this is the walk to keep that torch for London burning bright!

Please Note: There are several short flights of steps on this tour which cannot be avoided.

What's included

Walking Tour

Highlights

London has more than its fair share of strange tales, and on this walk you will be journeying into the depths of the old City to discover the more extraordinary stories and locations which are often overlooked.

name: Churchill and World War II : a Private Tour
description:
An informative and at times moving walk looking at the history of World War II looking especially at Winston Churchill but also others who contributed to the war effort and in some cases paid the ultimate sacrifice.
Duration

90 minutes

About

Embark on a private tour into the heart of London, a city forever intertwined with the life and legacy of Winston Churchill. Guided by your qualified London guide, Maggie / Philip, traverse the corridors of history to understand this towering figure's connection to the city, particularly the district of Westminster, where he lived, worked, and is now remembered.

Journey back in time to the places that played a significant role in Churchill's life, from the Palace of Westminster, the stage for his most riveting speeches, to the quaint St Margaret's Church, where he exchanged wedding vows. This exclusive walk will offer insights into Churchill's political career, his two terms as Prime Minister, and the rare honour of a Lying In State and State Funeral - events that were deeply etched in the annals of London.

In Parliament Square, stand in front of Churchill's statue, a monument with a story as compelling as the man himself. Learn about its controversial beginnings and triumphant unveiling, then stroll to the Old Yard of the Palace of Westminster, where World War II was declared. Here, we delve into the era when Chu
highlights: See the sights around Westminster that Winston Churchill knew
Follow in the footsteps of Winston Churchill
Qualified London tour guide
Our online calendar reflects the most up-to-date availability for private tours.

Your safety remains your responsibility at all times during the tour.

name: A Silent Disco Adventure in Newcastle
description:
It's a Flash Mob, it's a Silent Disco, it’s a roaming, rhythmic riot through Newcastle!

Join the Silent Adventures Team as we take you on a journey of song and dance through the famous streets of Newcastle. This musically instructed adventure will have you strutting your funky stuff and showing off your pop star voices as we serenade the people of Newcastle! Hi-tech headsets will fill your body with beats, theme songs and your favourite hits from across the decades. As your inhibitions evaporate and laughter takes over, you will be part of the best all dancing, all singing walking tour in town!

Ganging up with your super-stylish leader at the starting point, you’ll groove down the historic streets, passing famous landmarks, performers and the public looking on in awe and envy!

Your moves are awesome. You just don’t know it yet!

What's included

You'll be checked-in, given a set of our Silent Disco Headphones and taken on a 60 minute host-guided dance & flash-mobbing trek through the city!

Additional information
FAQs
Cancellations

name: A Silent Disco Adventure in Manchester
description:
It's a Flash Mob, it's a Silent Disco, it’s a roaming, rhythmic riot through Manchester!

Are you ready for the funnest, funniest, funkiest hour of your life?!

Join this unique, award-winning, five star Silent Disco Adventure tour through the iconic streets of Manchester on a musical adventure of Epic proportions! We'll have you singing like you do in the shower, dancing like you do at home, and having a good old laugh with our team of groovy guides, and hysterical host! Super hi-tech headphones will take you back through the decades, with some well known, banging old school tunes from back in the day, to chart-topping numbers of today! Make the city of Manchester your own personal dance-floor because it's time to Party!

You'll begin your Silent Disco Adventure with an introduction from your host with the most to get you warmed up, and then set off on your Silent Adventure to serenade Manchester and show the city something it has definitely never seen or heard before. A riot of flash-mobbery that you didn't even know you were a part of - Manchester is your stage now and it's time to dance!

The party is about to begin

name: River Rafting Safari (Individuals)
description:
This activity runs all year round.

Enjoy a 4-mile paddle through stunning river scenery while tackling a series of *Grade 2 rapids along the way.
Each raft is guided by one of our experienced Raft Guides, whose role is to safely navigate you downstream while ensuring everyone has a fun, memorable experience.

Perfect for families and friends, stag & hen parties, and work events, rafting is the ultimate team adventure. No previous experience is required — just a sense of adventure and a willingness to get involved.

*Please note: this river rafting experience includes low-grade rapids. Rapid size and intensity vary depending on river levels. During the summer months, rapids are typically smaller and more technical, while higher winter water levels can increase the excitement.

What's included
highlights: All equipment including Rafts, Paddles, Buoyancy Aids, Helmets, Wetsuits / Waterproofs
Transport from our hub to your activity start point and back
Photos and video which we'll share with you on the day
Our experienced guides who are guaranteed to give you the best time on the water
You will need to bring a pair of trainers you don't mind getting wet and muddy.
Please bring a towel or changing robe for changing in and out of your wetsuits.

name: 牛津校友伴你行 - 牛津步行导览 & 撑篙游船之旅（与他人共享导游）
description:
牛津是一座值得细细探索的城市，而真正理解它，需要同时从街道与河流两个角度体验。

这项 牛津步行导览与撑篙组合行程 将 90 分钟步行导览 与 River Cherwell 查韦尔河传统撑篙体验结合在一起，带来一次完整的牛津大学探索之旅。

行程首先从步行导览开始。

跟随牛津大学校友的脚步，由专业校友讲解员带领深入牛津大学核心区域。

这不仅仅是简单的景点讲解。

您将真正了解牛津大学今天如何运作，包括：

学院制度（Collegiate System）

导师制教学（Tutorial Method）

真实的申请与录取情况

学生生活与传统

在步行过程中，您将看到牛津最具代表性的地标，包括：

Radcliffe Camera 拉德克利夫图书馆

Bodleian Library 博德利图书馆

Sheldonian Theatre 谢尔登剧院

Bridge of Sighs 叹息桥

同时还会经过历史学院庭院、古老大门以及一些隐藏街角，感受牛津大学城市独特的氛围。

步行导览结束后，行程将在水上继续展开。

在 River Cherwell 切尔韦尔河 上乘坐传统撑篙船，缓缓经过 Christ Church Meadows 基督堂学院草坪 与多所学院花园，欣赏牛津最优美的自然景观。

这些河岸风景也与牛津文学历史紧密相连。例如：

Lewis Carroll 刘易斯·卡罗尔（《爱丽丝梦游仙境》作者）曾在 Christ Church 基督堂学院任教

Oscar Wilde 奥斯卡·王尔德 曾在
highlights: Stay Hydrated: Bring a water bottle and drink plenty of water, especially in warm weather. (Please note though that there are no toilet stops on the tour)
Wear Comfortable Shoes: Ensure your footwear is suitable for walking and provides good support.
Protect Yourself from the Sun: Wear sunscreen, a hat, and sunglasses.
Be Weather Prepared: Check the weather forecast and dress appropriately. Bring a raincoat or umbrella if needed.
Stay with the Group: Do not stray from the group, and always stay within sight of the tour guide.
Follow Instructions: Listen to the tour guide’s instructions and adhere to any safety warnings given.
Mind Your Step: Be cautious of uneven surfaces, curbs, and potential trip hazards.
Traffic Awareness: Use pedestrian crossings and be mindful of vehicles and bicycles, especially in busy areas.

name: School group: Private Great Fire of London Tour
description:
Step back in time to one of the most dramatic moments in London’s history: the Great Fire of 1666. On this captivating guided walk, you will follow in the footsteps of Samuel Pepys and others who witnessed the destruction of three-quarters of the city, forever altering its landscape.

Led by a London tour guide, this tour takes you through the narrow streets and historic landmarks that bore witness to the inferno. Learn how the fire began in a humble bakery on Pudding Lane before quickly engulfing churches, homes, and businesses. Discover the panic, heroism, and devastation that unfolded over four days as the City burned, and find out who was blamed for the disaster.

This tour also explores the lasting impact of the fire, from the introduction of new building regulations to the reconstruction of St Paul’s Cathedral under Sir Christopher Wren. Hear fascinating tales of survival, loss, and resilience, and uncover how some Londoners took advantage of the chaos for their own gain.

Perfect for history lovers and curious explorers alike, this immersive experience will bring the Gre
highlights: A focused introduction to one of London’s most famous disasters, ideal for History, Geography and Citizenship groups.
Site‑specific stories about Samuel Pepys, Londoners, and key figures of the 1666 fire, helping students visualise life in the city and how events unfolded.
Discussion prompts on urban planning, fire prevention, and the social, economic and architectural impact of the fire on London’s rebuilding.
Meeting Point – Arrive at Monument Station (exact details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in; the tour departs promptly to keep to your schedule.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water; notebooks are recommended for jotting down historical facts or observations.
Accessibility – Route includes cobbled streets and uneven surfaces; please contact us in advance to discuss specific access needs or adaptations.
Walk through the streets of the City of London where the Great Fire of 1666 began and spread, exploring key sites including Pudding Lane and the path of destruction.

name: School group: Private Oliver Twist Tour
description:
This tour follows in the footsteps of Oliver Twist through London’s streets, exploring locations that illuminate crime, poverty, workhouses and childhood in Victorian Britain. Students connect key scenes and characters to the real environments that inspired Dickens.

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A GCSE English Literature‑focused walk designed around “Oliver Twist”, supporting close reading and contextual understanding.
Emphasis on setting, social context, narrative voice and Dickens’s critique of the Poor Law, crime and respectability.
Opportunities for short tasks, quotation recall and discussion linked to exam‑style questions and assessment objectives.
** Tour Reviews:**

Fiona (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Hazel is a brilliant, knowledgeable and engaging guide. Thoroughly enjoyable and strongly recommended.

Jan (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
A delightful and informative walk with ace Guide Hazel. So many insights into Victorian society
highlights: A GCSE English Literature‑focused walk designed around “Oliver Twist”, supporting close reading and contextual understanding.
Emphasis on setting, social context, narrative voice and Dickens’s critique of the Poor Law, crime and respectability.
Opportunities for short tasks, quotation recall and discussion linked to exam‑style questions and assessment objectives.
Meeting Point – Clear landmark close to the opening section of the route, with joining instructions sent ahead of your visit.
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes before the start time so students can be grouped and behaviour expectations set.
What to Bring – Comfortable shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing, water, copies of the text if desired and notebooks for quotations and prompts.
Accessibility – Some sections may include uneven pavements, steps or narrow alleys; possible adjustments can be discussed in advance.
Trace Oliver’s London, visiting streets and districts that reflect the worlds of workhouses, slums, middle‑class respectability and the criminal underworld.

name: History of England Tour in 2 Hours with Royal Thames Cruise
description:
Discover the complete story of England on this fully guided 2-hour walking tour designed to give visitors a clear, engaging, and chronological understanding of English history. Led by an expert local guide, this immersive experience traces England’s journey from Roman Britain through Saxon and Norman rule, medieval monarchy, religious change, empire, and the formation of the modern English state.
This immersive experience begins with a 2-hour guided walking tour offering an accessible and engaging introduction to the history of England. Starting outside St Paul’s Cathedral, guests are introduced to Roman Britain, early Christianity, and the long relationship between religion and power in English history.

What's included

Comprehensive overview of English history from ancient to modern times

name: Liverpool Beatles Walking Tour
description:
Discover how The Beatles became The Beatles - in the city where it all began.

This small-group walking tour starts at Liverpool’s iconic waterfront and follows a carefully designed route through the streets that shaped the band’s rise. Rather than simply visiting famous locations, your expert guide brings together the people, places and moments that turned a local group into a global phenomenon.

From the influence of the docks and the Mersey Ferry to Brian Epstein’s record store and the Cavern Club, this is a connected story of Liverpool’s music scene — and the role it played in changing music history.

With a maximum of 12 guests, the experience remains personal, engaging and rich in detail throughout.

What's included

Story-led experience – understand how the Beatles happened, not just where they went

Small groups (max 12 guests) – more interaction, better guiding

name: Bleeding Hearts and Body Parts Walk
description:
London’s history is steeped in blood, betrayal, and mystery. Join us on a journey through Farringdon and Smithfield, where the echoes of public executions, rebellions, and gruesome crimes still haunt the streets.

Beneath the historic streets of Farringdon and Smithfield lies a past stained with blood. This was a place of disease, rebellion, and religious turmoil—where martyrs were burned, criminals met their end, and the unfortunate simply disappeared. The cries of the condemned once rang out from execution sites, while plague pits swallowed thousands of nameless dead.

On this chilling journey, we peel back the layers of history to reveal London’s dark heart, where cold-blooded murder, public executions, and the dismembered remains of the past tell the
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at Farringdon Station (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your assigned guide.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water.
Accessibility – The walk includes some very narrow walkways, steps and cobblestoneNo Step-free route available.If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Introduction & Welcome - A warm welcome and an overview of what to expect on the tour. Brief historical context of Farringdon and Smithfield.
Public Executions at Smithfield - Stand where London’s condemned met their fate. Learn about gruesome executions, from heretics burned at the stake to traitors hanged and quartered.
William Wallace & the Peasants' Revolt - Discover how a revolt against the monarchy ended in bloodshed.
Bleeding Heart Yard & the Lady in Red - Hear the chilling legend of Lady Elizabeth Hatton. Uncover why her still-beating heart was allegedly found on these streets.

name: Canterbury Hidden City Tour
description:
The Old Buttermarket Pub, 39 Burgate, Canterbury CT1 2HW

2 hours • Ages 18+ • 10am & 3pm

Discover Canterbury's Hidden Secrets
Duration

2 hours

About

The Tour is based on the History of the City, and its architecture it also covers several aspects of English Language, and the origins of phrases we take for granted.

The duration is around 2 hours, and it's a gentle walking pace around the city. Canterbury's history stretched back over the millennia, from pre-roman settlement to the present day city bustling with visitors, students, and locals alike. The footsteps of Pilgrims and parsons are etched into its fabric and the tour takes you in their footsteps. The tour is fully accessible for anyone in a wheelchair or with mobility issues, which given my work with the elderly I feel it's important to be inclusive.

As it's a walking tour, on city streets, just be sure to wear comfortable walking shoes and be sure to dress for the English Climate, i.e. on a sunny day bring an umbrella, just in case.

I am very pleased to announce that the book to accompany the tour is now available to download in 'Kindle Fire' Format or in 'Paperback' format from Amazon worldwide. A link will be provided in your confirmation email.
I look forward to showing you around my beautiful city.
It makes a perfect souvenir too.

Kind Regards
John

name: Full-Day Private "All Creatures Great & Small" Tour
description: This Private Day Tour provides the opportunity to explore the Yorkshire Dales National Park, but with a particular focus on the countryside and locations used for filming the new Channel 5 / PBS 'All Creatures Great & Small' TV series starring Nicholas Ralph in the lead role of Yorkshire Vet, James Herriot. You will have photo stops in Barden, Malham, Arncliffe, Hawkswick and Burnsall and a longer stop in Grassington, used as the main filming location for "Darrowby". You will also have the option to visit the award-winning World of James Herriot Visitor Attraction in Thirsk to see where Alf Wight (the real James Herriot) lived, worked and wrote his series of books, based upon his experiences as a young Yorkshire Vet. If you do want to visit the World of James Herriot, we will take you there first in the morning as this sets the scene nicely for the rest of the tour. If not, you can spend the additional time enjoying more of the Yorkshire Dales National Park – the choice is yours and can be agreed with your Driver in the morning! Tour departs 9.00am and drops off 5.15pm.

name: Annual Pass Admission to Newcastle Castle
description:
The Gateway to Old Newcastle

All tickets are now valid for 365 days from the date of purchase. Buy once, visit many times at no extra cost.

Last entry is at 4pm

About

Visit our family-friendly historic attraction to walk in the steps of the people who lived and died in the shadow of Newcastle Castle.

Steeped in history this imposing Norman fortress, in the centre of Newcastle upon Tyne, is a rugged reminder of northern England's turbulent history. This was no baron's stately home. Newcastle Castle is a grim reminder of royal authority where armies gathered and criminals were imprisoned and executed. It is where the story of Newcastle began, the reason the city got its name and has the most commanding views over the city and the River Tyne.

A great visit no matter the weather, you can easily reach u
highlights: The tickets offer the freedom and flexibility to explore the Castle at your leisure, coming back as many times as you’d like to see all your favourite bits.
Families can have a recurring fun day out during the holidays or weekend without having to pay multiple times.
Annual tickets holders can sign up for the annual ticket newsletter, which will contain exclusive discounts on events and on-goings at the Castle.
UK taxpayers can choose to gift aid their ticket, which means we will receive and extra cut of the ticket sale at no extra cost to you.

name: Call the Midwife Tour
description:
Walking tour and experience that takes you behind the scenes of this much loved show. It includes original props and costumes from the show itself. Working in partnership with the creators of Call the Midwife, Neal Street Productions, the Call the Midwife Tour is overseen by the show’s producers and features material from the latest series, as well as props, costumes, set and of course a costumed Midwife!

Originally based on the series of books written by Jennifer Worth, the award winning TV adaptation of Call the Midwife has attracted fans worldwide since it first aired in 2012. Whilst the Nonnatus House scenes are filmed at a closed studio set in Surrey, many of the exterior scenes and other sites are filmed at Chatham Dockyard in Kent.

Watched by more than 10 million viewers and now approaching its eighth series, the Historic D

About

Make the most of your first visit to London

Venture through the royal City of Westminster, and the financial City of London and fun Southwark. This full day introductory tour is perfect for first-time visitors. Not only will you explore iconic London landmarks you will also experience a London bus / London Underground. Accompanied by a professional guide, learn about the history of this area as you snap memorable photos.

As a Londoner, your guide will make your tour special by taking you to some of the lesser known/visited spots.

See Big Ben (or can you?) and the gothic Houses of Parliament. Discover the origins of the gunpowder plot and why the English celebrate Bonfire Night.

Take in the spectacular views of Westminster Abbey where every king and queen has been crowned since 1066.

Wander through a Royal Park which has been the location for many films and TV series. Take in the wonder of Buckingham Palace and learn how it became the formal residence of reigning monarchs.

name: Private Secrets of Belgravia
description:
Belgravia is one of the most expensive places to live in London. Hear how an area of swampland became ripe for development, which eventually became the must have address for the members of the ruling class.

Actors and TV celebrities have long made a beeline for Belgravia when searching for a London home.

Joan Collins, Sir Michael Caine, Sir Lawrence Olivier, Christopher Lee and Vivien Leigh have all lived in Belgravia plus many more notables.

More recently, Belgravia has attracted Noam Gottesman, founder of Mayfair hedge fund GLG, investment guru George Soros, advertising and art dealing millionaire Charles Saatchi and his wife Nigella Lawson plus CarpetRight founder Lord Harris of Peckham.

Hidden backstreet pubs, quaint cobbled streets and garden squares add to the character of this exclusive area.

Impressive mansions and townhouses take pride of place, but also there was also a large domestic servant population that worked tirelessly to keep the owners households running.

name: Private Liverpool 3-hour Must Sees Tour!
description:
About

Get your bearings and discover Liverpool on an exclusive private tour, with your own local, expert guide. The best way to maximise your time in Liverpool, get to know a local person and enjoy discovering our city!

Immerse yourself in the sights and sounds of the city as you travel back through the centuries to discover our turbulent history, and how it has shaped Liverpool into the vibrant place you see today. Extend the trip and see even more sights in 3 hours!

On this 3 hour tour, you'll not only see and learn about our history, but you’ll also gain an authentic insight into modern life in the city today and explore some hidden gems along the way! From Liverpool’s trading history that created its foundations, to the titanic and of course, the Beatles!

There’s something for everyone on this unique experience.

Pier Head & The Waterfront
The Albert Docks

The Bridewell

And many hidden treasures!

name: Freeroam VR Arena
description:
WHAT IS FREEROAM VR?

Free-roam VR is a world similar to the real one, where players can observe simply by wearing a VR headset. With their controllers they can see their augmented hands and bodies in the virtual world and interact with the objects within the game. Its played with wireless VR headsets and a group will walk around an "arena" playspace rather than stand still.

WHAT GAMES CAN I PLAY?

In Portsmouth upstairs (no lift access), we have a choice of Free-Roam Arena games. Most require 2 players. Some support upto six players, some are capped at four players.

We have content from VERSUS (Laser Tag) and Hero Zone which has a few titles including a cartoon style AMAZING zombie experience for all ages called Dead Ahead…

During a session each game lasts around 10 mins, so you can either repeat or try at least two from the list.

During a longer sessions you can repeat levels more or try even more of the games!!

VERSUS LASER TAG

Versus is a retro-futuristic VR laser tag. Jump into the arena for PVP battles with your V-99 blaster.

GAMEPLAY

The rules are simple: Players are in a competitive deathmatch arena, a 6-minute battle where the highest score wins. 1v1 or 2v2 or 4 player free for all fights in Versus Arena are intense and fun!

MAPS

6 Maps with virtual walls, barricades an

description:
Embrace the art of slow craft with Nottingham Lace

Choose from collection of beautiful, locally made Nottingham Lace and embellish with stitch, appliqué and beadwork to create a one of kind Lace hoop.

About our Nottingham Lace

We work with ninth generation Lever’s Lace producers, Cluny Lace, for our superior quality and intricately patterned Nottingham Lace fabrics. These are inspired by design ideas dating back to the 1600’s. Based just eight miles away from our Lace Market design studio and gallery.

Notably in 2012, Cluny Lace provided the Royal School of Needlework at Hampton Court Palace with lace to produce Kate Middleton’s royal lace appliqué for her veil and shoes – for her wedding to Prince William, Duke of Cambridge.

This is a fun Bushcraft session. Learn to make a fire using just a flint & Steel, boil some water on your fire to make a hot drink. Once the fire is going we will use knife skills to craft some bush bling. Make some beads, a tent peg, whistle or even start carving a spoon..

name: Mountain Biking: Private Half Day Ride with a Guide
description:
Ready to take your mountain biking to the next level? Our 1:1 Guided Ride offers personalised coaching designed to help you develop your riding skills, improve your technique, and gain confidence while exploring some of the best trails Exmoor has to offer.

We can also offer a 1:2 session if you would like a private session for couples or partners, meaning you get to take the ride at a pace you're both comfortable with. Add £25 for each rider.

Whether you’re looking to master the basics or tackle more advanced riding techniques, this tailored session will focus on your specific needs. You’ll be guided by an experienced British Cycling MTB Leader and Coach, who will provide expert tips, hands-on coaching, and plenty of encouragement as you ride through Exmoor’s breathtaking landscapes.

name: Private Liverpool City Centre Walking Tour
description:
Get your bearings and discover Liverpool on an exclusive private tour, with your own local, expert guide.

Over 1.5 hours, you’ll not only uncover Liverpool’s past but also get an authentic glimpse into what it means to be a Liverpudlian today. Along the way, explore hidden gems and iconic landmarks, from the legendary docks and the city’s Titanic connections to the famous mythical Liver Birds and, of course, The Beatles! Whether you're a history buff, music lover, or just curious about the city's soul, there's something for everyone.

The tour includes:

Pier Head & The Waterfront
The Albert Docks
The Three Graces
The Cavern Club
And many hidden treasures!

Price: £99 for a group of up to 4, then only £15pp

Duration: 1.5 hours

Meeting point: by the Museum of Liverpool - if you'd like your guide to meet you elsewhere in the city, just pop us an email!

Dates & Times: Available every day at a time that suits you!

Tour extension: you can extend your tour to 2 hours in the booking form! Only £30 extra per group.

Cancellations

name: Composers of the West End
description:
A music-themed private tour, touching on the lighter side, about composers connected with the Strand and some of the places where their music was performed.

Not only does this private walking tour cover a variety of classical composers but also theatre and opera productions plus some world-class performance venues in London's  West End.

Hear how European influences, including  George Frederic Handel and other notable composers helped secure the future of London's increasingly sophisticated classical music scene and how that created popular offshoots in operetta and musicals.

Enjoy walking through Regency London with its wide straight streets and square. Marvel at the neoclassical theatre designed by architect John Nash before embarking on the Victorian centre for entertainment before finishing in iconic Covent Garden.

name: Zombie 3D Archery Dorset
description:
Book Online - We sell out!

This is not your standard archery session! 3D Zombie Archery at our Dorset woodland is a masterclass in stealth and shooting skills, honing your archery skills to precision and accuracy.
You’ll learn all you need to know on an archery training session, then head deep into the woods, take shots from elevation, longer distances and through vegetation. Here you’ll put your new knowledge to the test as you keep your eyes peeled, moving stealthily through the forest and firing at Zombie themed targets.

name: Private Leeds Dark Side Tour
description:
The perfect way to feel some spooky vibes on your trip to Leeds, and explore the real dark side of our city.

Uncover Leeds's secrets and skeletons; from alien abductions and Leeds' very own mummy, to the ghosts that haunt the abbey and gruesome deaths, this tour has it all. This 1.5 hour special tour will bring our dark past into light as you discover a unique side to our iconic city.

Throughout its history, Leeds has had many a murder and ghost story; some centuries ago, some only decades and some even today. You never know what or who you might encounter.

Tour highlights:

The Leeds Mummy
The first recorded murder in Leeds
The ladies of Temple Newsam
The skull and stones yard
The haunted pubs of Leeds
…& much more!

Price: £99 for a group of up to 4 then only £15pp!

Duration: 1.5 hours

Start time & location: choose your start time in the booking form. The tour will start outside Leeds Art Gallery in Victoria Square.

name: London Loo Tour
description:
Join your guide and be immersed (metaphorically, that is!) on a whirlwind tour of the history of the British public toilet. From the earliest latrines through to the future of flushing you may never think about a simple visit to the lavatory the same way again!The perfect day out for anyone who likes to dive a bit deeper (metaphorically again!)….  lifelong Londoners and first time visitors alike!

Our classic route runs from Waterloo to the West End, exploring the potty politics of public toilets. From stories of intrigue to practical tips, this tour is an investment in your bladder future! Never be caught short again!

name: A Silent Disco Adventure in Liverpool
description:
It's a Flash Mob, it's a Silent Disco, it’s a roaming, rhythmic riot through Liverpool!

Music is the life-blood of Liverpool. It seeps from the souls of Scousers, flows into the River Mersey, thunders out to sea… and changes the world!

Silent Adventures invites you on a unique roaming riot of revelry around the sound-soaked streets of the music capital of the UK. Expect all your inhibitions to evaporate away as you don our hi-tech headsets and bust some swinging grooves whilst our amazing Silent Adventure Guides take you on a one hour adventure of this amazing city. You’ll never look at Liverpool, or yourselves, in the same way again!

Your moves are awesome, you just don't know it yet.

What's included
You'll be checked-in, given a set of our Silent Disco Headphones and taken on a 60 minute host-guided dance & flash-mobbing trek through the city!

Join the Silent Adventures Team as we take you on a journey of song and dance through the famous streets of London. This musically instructed adventure aims to have your funky moves on display and your pop star voices serenading the people of the capital! Hi-tech headsets will fill your body with beats, theme songs and your favourite hits from across the decades. As your inhibitions evaporate and laughter takes over, you will be part of the best all-dance, all-singing walking tour in town!

Ganging up with your super-stylish leader at the starting point, you’ll groove down the historic streets, passing famous landmarks, performers and the public - looking on in awe and jealousy!

name: Oxford On Screen Walking Tour
description:
Join us for an Oxford On Screen Walking Tour

Oxford naturally lends itself as a stunning backdrop for the movie and TV screen and reflects why VisitBritain latest promotion campaign is called “Starring Great Britain”.

The city has been the host of over 40 movies and 7 TV series, and is synonymous with 102 episodes of Inspector Morse, Lewis and Endeavour. The city and its historical university naturally lend itself as the inspiration and backdrop for many stories on screen.

Our expert and passionate guides will show you around the most famous landmarks that have been immortalised on screen, from the Radcliffe Camera in Emerald Fennel’s Saltburn, to the Bodleian Library in Harry Potter, and share the titbits and anecdotes that filming in Oxford can bring.

Tread in Inspector Morse’s footsteps around the pubs of Oxford, follow Lyra (His Dark Materials) around the colleges, and eat with the vampires (A Discovery of Witches) in the historic Covered Market.

Suitable for all ages, whether you are a “set-jetting” cinephile

description:
Explore magical London like never before on one of our magical, private walking tours! Follow in the footsteps of Harry, Ron, and Hermione as we visit key filming locations and the real-life inspirations behind the Harry Potter series.

With flexible start times and group sizes, on a private tour you can tailor the tour to your schedule and preferences. You also have the exclusive option to add an extra stop at Platform 9 ¾, and we can even send over a personalised Hogwarts acceptance letter for any children in your tour group.

description:
After a short bus trip to join the Trail at Carluddon we walk on rugged terrain across moorland tops and wooded valleys, crossing streams and seeing evidence of the former industrial use of this area.
There is an optional café stop at Wheal Martyn before walking along scenic disused railway tracks back to St Austell.

name: Charlestown South West Coast Path guided walk
description:
We will walk a beautiful 3-mile section of this, England's longest National Trail, gently rising over clifftops and passing rocky coves, sandy beaches and the exquisite historic harbour of Charlestown.

There's a wealth of wildlife to spot, stunning views, industrial heritage and lots more. After a short bus ride we begin our walk at Porthpean and pass the site of an old gun battery before descending and getting our first sight of Charlestown Harbour from above. After passing through the historic harbour at Charlestown, there are soon expansive views over St Austell Bay. The path clings closely to the edge of low cliffs adjacent to a golf course. We leave the coast path above Carlyon Bay to cross the golf course and return to the start point.
highlights: Coastal walking with little ascent
Passes through Charlestown historic harbour
Superb views across St Austell Bay
Friendly guiding style - questions encouraged
A Coast Path Friendly event giving £1 back to the Trail

single bus fare Par Market to Duporth

name: Lansallos Coast Path guided walk
description:
A short bus trip to join the Coast Path at Lansallos then walk on rugged terrain across cliff tops, crossing streams and skirting above the stunning Lantic Bay with its white sands and turquoise waters.

There are several moderate climbs (mostly with steps), totalling 360m of ascent.

Pencarrow gives expansive views across St Austell Bay to Dodman Point and the Lizard on a clear day. Looking east, there are views towards Polperro and beyond.

Optional visit to the well-preserved 14th century blockhouse at the end of this 4.5 mile hike, time permitting.
highlights: Coastal walking with several significant ascents
A Coast Path Friendly event giving £1 back to the Trail
Friendly guiding style - questions encouraged

name: Fowey Coast Path guided walk
description:
A short bus trip to join the Coast Path at Polmear (NB on Bank Holidays this will be substituted with a cross country walk to Polkerris) then walk on rugged terrain across low cliff tops, crossing streams and visiting the tiny harbour of Polkerris.

Many connections with Daphne du Maurier and her novels can be made on this headland.

Climbing higher, with expansive views across St Austell Bay to Gribbin Head then returning via Polridmouth Cove to Fowey, with a castle visit at the end of this 6 mile walk, time permitting.

name: Family Friendly Natural History Museum of London Guided Tour - Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Explore one of London’s most fascinating museums on this private, family-friendly tour of the Natural History Museum. With an expert guide leading the way, you’ll discover highlights from the museum’s incredible collection—from ancient fossils and towering skeletons to groundbreaking scientific discoveries—through engaging storytelling that’s suited for both kids and adults.

Begin beneath the soaring ceiling of Hintze Hall, where the 25-meter-long blue whale skeleton, known as Hope, captures the scale and power of the natural world. In the Earth Hall, meet Sophie the Stegosaurus, one of the most complete dinosaur skeletons ever discovered. Along the way, uncover the story of Cheddar Man, Britain’s oldest known human, and see a first edition of Darwin’s On the Origin of Species—the book that forever changed how we understand life on Earth.

Designed to be both educational and accessible, this tour offers a memorable journey through the museum’
highlights:
Hope the Blue Whale
Marvel at Hope, the 25.2-meter-long blue whale skeleton, suspended majestically from the ceiling of Hintze Hall.

Archaeopteryx Fossil
One of the most significant fossils ever found, this 150-million-year-old specimen provides crucial evidence of the evolutionary link between dinosaurs and birds.

First Edition of On the Origin of Species
A cornerstone of scientific thought, this 1859 first edition of On the Origin of Species is preserved in the museum’s Treasures Gallery.

name: Newcastle True Crime Tour
description:
Join our local expert on a romp through Newcastle’s history of crime, executions and tales of debauchery. Among others, we will explore tales of witchcraft, the lucrative trade of body-snatchers or a prank gone badly wrong.

Begin your tour from the heart of the city at Earl Grey’s Monument and take a walk on the dark side of Newcastle & revisit scenes of murder, intrigue & suspense that rattled this Tyneside city.

Locations include Newcastle’s former jails, places of execution and courtrooms, where we retell the most infamous court cases and we shall re-visit actual crime scenes of days gone by.

name: School group: Private A Christmas Carol Tour
description:
This tour traces Ebenezer Scrooge’s London through the City streets Dickens knew best, illuminating key scenes, settings and social context for “A Christmas Carol”. Students connect quotations and themes to real locations associated with poverty, commerce, charity and Victorian change.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A GCSE English Literature‑focused walk designed around “A Christmas Carol”, with clear support for close reading and contextual understanding.​
Strong emphasis on setting, narrative structure, characterisation and social/historical context, including workhouses, charity, business and reform.​
Opportunities for on‑the‑spot discussion, short tasks and quotation recall linked to exam‑style questions.​

name: Private Changing the Guard Tour
description:
Our more exclusive Changing the Guard tour!

This is the London walking tour which will ensure you are in the best position possible to see the extraordinary Changing the Guard ceremony while avoiding the crowds at the gates of Buckingham Palace. Rather than standing still for hours, we see various stages of the ceremony, including the inspection, Old Guard, New Guard, Palaces, and even march alongside the Guards and Ceremonial Bands!

Not only is this tour private and exclusive to your group, we start a little earlier so that we can see the Horse Guards come on duty as well. And then the tour lasts for an extra half an hour so we can see the entirety of the ceremony from start to finish (except the section in the courtyard of Buckingham Palace which is far too crowded!)

We can tailor the tour to your requirements - just let your guide know at the start of the tour.

name: Changing of the Guard Guided Walking Tour – Private (ENGLISH)
description:
Experience one of Britain’s most iconic traditions on this private walking tour through historic Westminster. With an expert guide, uncover the history behind the Changing of the Guard Ceremony as you witness this spectacular display of pageantry and precision.

Begin in the monumental Trafalgar Square, then follow the Royal Guard as they march in full ceremonial dress, accompanied by the rhythmic beat of military drums. Admire their perfectly groomed horses and distinctiv uniforms—scarlet tunics and towering bearskin hats—as you pass through Admiralty Arch, past St. James’s Palace, the most senior royal residence in Britain, and onto the historic parade grounds. Your guide will share stories of Britain’s most revered monarchs as the New Guard replaces the Old Guard in this centuries-old tradition. Conclude your tour with views of Buckingham Palace, where the guards arrive at their final post.

This tour offers a captivating glimpse into B
highlights:
Trafalgar Square
Discover London’s most famous public square, a historic gathering place named after Britain’s greatest naval victory at the Battle of Trafalgar (1805).

Changing of the Guard Parade
Experience this centuries-old military tradition as the King’s Guards march from St. James’s Palace to Wellington Barracks, dressed in full ceremonial uniform.

St. James’s Palace (Exterior Only)
See Britain’s oldest royal palace, built by Henry VIII in the 1530s, which remains an active residence and holds official royal events to this day.

Horse Guards Parade
Watch the formal guard inspection at the Horse Guards Parade, where the Household Cavalry maintains a 365-day-a-year presence, protecting royal residences.

name: First Class Return Travel in  Queen Victoria's Carriage  with Afternoon Tea
description:
Feel Royal for the day! Travel in Queen Victoria’s Royal Saloon while being served a fresh Afternoon Tea with Prosecco on arrival.

Embsay and Bolton Abbey Steam Railway and Stately Trains are proud to offer the unique opportunity of a journey in Queen Victoria’s saloon.

Built for the London and South Western Railway’s Royal Train in 1885 it was altered to become Queen Victoria’s personal coach in her Golden Jubilee year, 1887. She used it to travel from Waterloo to Gosport from where she journied on the Royal Yacht to the Isle of Wight to Osborne House. Several European heads of state used the coach when making a state visit to Britain.

Following Queen Victoria’s death in 1901 it continued in Royal use for several years before being converted to a family saloon and eventually being sold as a body by the Southern Railway in 1930. From then until the 1980’s it was a home for a gamekeeper before being moved t

name: Southwark Walk: Pilgrims, Playwrights, and Prisoners
description:
Beyond the bustling stalls of Borough Market lies a fascinating district packed with stories of trade, theatre, and resilience. Southwark has long been a gateway to London, a place where medieval pilgrims gathered, Shakespearean theatre thrived, and some of the city’s most intriguing figures left their mark.

This 90-minute guided walk uncovers hidden histories, literary connections, and remnants of the past, revealing the area's significance beyond its modern-day vibrancy. Discover how Southwark became the entertainment hub of Tudor London, with its playhouses, taverns, and bear-baiting pits drawing crowds from both sides of the river. Hear how Shakespeare found inspiration here, creating works that would define English theatre, and how his rivals sought to outshine him.

The area has a rich literary past, featuring in The Canterbury Tales, Little Dorrit, and David Copperfield, with traces of these stories still visible in the streets today. Explore a surviving 17th-century galleried inn mentioned by Dickens, and find out why pilgrims, playwrights, and prisoners all had their place in Southwark’s past.

Along the way, we will uncover the lost world of Southwark’s ancient taverns, it
