Accessibility – The walk includes some cobbled streets and uneven surfaces. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
Toilets & Refreshments – Public toilets are available at London Bridge Station and Borough Market. You may wish to explore Borough Market before or after the tour for food and drinks.
Introduction – A warm welcome and an overview of Southwark’s 2,000-year history.
Hidden Histories of Southwark – Discover the lesser-known stories behind this fascinating area.
Literary Connections – Hear about Chaucer, Dickens, and Shakespeare, and their links to Southwark.

name: Private Windsor Town Tour with Skip-the-Line Windsor Castle Tickets!
description:
Discover the royal town of Windsor before stepping inside the oldest and largest occupied castle in the world.

Take Windsor at your own pace, spend quality time with a local and get your bearings on your very own private tour of Windsor. This experience combines a guided walking tour of Windsor with expert historical insight into Windsor Castle before you enter, so when you go inside, everything you see has context, stories, and meaning.

Your guide will bring Windsor to life with tales of kings, queens, scandals, ceremonies, and centuries of royal drama, while showing you the town’s most important sights. Outside the castle, you’ll get a full introduction to its history, architecture, and the Royal Family’s connection to it today.

At the end of the tour, your guide will take you directly to the entrance, and assist you in entering the castle with your tickets (included in this tour price!) allowing you to explore the State Apartments, St George’s Chapel, and more at your own pace.

name: Private Manchester City Centre Tour
description:
Take the city at your own pace and discover Manchester on an exclusive private tour, with your own local, expert guide.

Immerse yourself in the sights and sounds of the city as you travel back through the centuries to discover our turbulent history, and how it has shaped Manchester into the vibrant place you see today.

From our Roman foundations to the industrial boom, from the fight for women’s rights to the thriving music and LGBTQ+ scenes of today — Manchester is a city that never stands still. On this guided tour with a local, walk in the footsteps of suffragettes, pioneering merchants, legendary street artists, and football icons. Explore the city’s most famous landmarks and uncover hidden gems along the way.

The tour includes:

St Peter's Square
Royal Exchange Theatre
Mediaeval Quarter
Street Art
Gay Village
Northern Quarter
And many hidden treasures!

Price: £99 for a group of up to 4 people, then only £15 per additional person.

name: Private Nottingham Walking Tour
description:
Get your bearings and discover Nottingham on an exclusive private tour, with your own local, expert guide. Our tour is all about spending time with a local, as well as uncovering our deep history.

This tour doesn't just cover the history—it’s about experiencing Nottingham through the eyes of those who shaped it. Wander through Nottingham’s oldest streets, uncovering stories of Anglo-Saxon settlers, medieval merchants, and daring outlaws. Learn how the Industrial Revolution transformed the city, explore its ancient cave system, and hear tales of Robin Hood, body snatchers, and revolutionary entrepreneurs who changed the course of history.

description:
A dining experience you wont forget

We will welcome you to 17th century Broadway, in the heart Broadway’s busy Coaching Era

Stop off at Broadway Museum & Art Gallery for a glass of traditional Ale, let us take you on a journey through coaching history, sharing tales of 17th c England to set your toes curling!

Churn your own butter, pat it, shape it and wrap it with our eco wrap to take away with you.

Treat yourself to a rustic mid morning or mid afternoon 17th century Hearty Stew or Ploughman’s (seasonally dependent) and freshly warmed bread (ready for your own freshly made butter), served and enjoyed the coaching way!

Choose to kayak or canoe… both are super stable and very user friendly, you'll pick up the art of paddling in no time.
You'll be guided by one of our experienced river guides who will teach you the skills you'll need to enjoy this five star kayaking experience.

name: Modern British Flavours London Foodie Adventure: +10 dishes and drinks
description:
Embark on a unique London food tour, starting at Seven Dials. Discover a hidden bakery in Neal's Yard for a delightful breakfast combo. Wander through Soho, exploring its rich culture and history. Enjoy acclaimed cuisine and the finest Guinness at a legendary pub. Taste traditional British fish and chips in a historical setting, and uncover the intriguing tales of London’s early red-light district.

Savor a pint and a savoury snack on the edge of Chinatown, concluding with a sweet treat in a quaint Soho spot. This tour blends delicious food, captivating stories, and rich history, perfect for those eager to explore London's vibrant culture and cuisine. Join us for an unforgettable journey!

As we meander through Soho, you'll experience the essence of London's diverse culture. Stop by an iconic pub, celebrated for its exceptional cuisine and the finest Guinness pour in town. Discover tales of Soho's vibrant past, from its legendary music studios to its revolutionary fashion streets.
highlights: Explore London with a local guide.

name: Armstrong and Austen in the Age of Empire: (Re)Inventing Society and Industry at Newcastle and Gibside
description:
We walk you through the changing urban landscape and industries of the city at the height of the British Empire, with stops at iconic engineering and architectural marvels, and the stunning neo-classical setting of nearby Gibside. Discover how visionary engineers reshaped the North-East with steam power and early electrical systems, challenging the social and economic status of its traditional elites.

Itinerary

We journey through key historical landmarks and sites in the city including Grainger Street, Monument, Forth Street, Central Station, the Swing Bridge and so on, to bring the stories of genius engineer William, Lord Armstrong, steam pioneers George and Robert Stephenson, aristocrat Charles, 2nd Earl Grey, and other key figures, inventions, and transformations from the period to life. I

name: Private Intimate Churchill's London & War Rooms Museum Tour
description:
Winston Churchill plotted our victory from the streets of London
On this deeply personal tour, we will visit the lesser-known streets, shops and buildings which played a huge role in Churchill's life and self-image. You'll check out the shop where he purchased his iconic cigars, stand in the military parade ground with its own hidden WW2 communications centre, visit the wine merchant with cellars running beneath the street, and take in the social clubs of which he was a member.

Hear about his relationships to his wife Clementine and President Roosevelt, his life as a young soldier, and the stories of valour and predestiny which shaped his journey to become the man of the hour.

And then for the mind blowing trip into the Churchill War Rooms. Here your guide will accompany you through London's most atmospheric and haunting museum, literally entering a time capsule from London in the War.

What's included

Visit the extraordinary Churchill War Rooms with your guide

Visit the cigar shop frequented by Winston Churchill

Walk through the extraordinary area of St James, famous for its clubs and high profile residents.

See the 500 year old Tudor pala

See the historical hat shop used by our Prime Minister

Take a more personal journey into Churchill's world

description:
The perfect way to enjoy a fun activity with your other half!
Rates

Couple: £132

2 hours

About

If you love little adventures together and like the idea of learning to surf in your own private surf lesson then our couples surf lessons are for you.

You’ll be met and welcomed by your surf coach at our central Newquay base where you can get changed and store your belongings.

After a short overview and introduction of the lesson, it’s time to jump into wetsuits and head to the beach.

We provide all the equipment for the session although you’ll need to bring something to wear under your wetsuit, a towel, and a pair of shoes for the beach.

Our aim for the lesson is to provide you with a fun environment to enjoy taking part in an activity together. Our private couples surf lessons are designed with you in mind, there’s no rush and we’ll break the lesson down into easy bite-size sections.

All equipment included!

Just bring a towel, shoes for the beach, and something to wear under a wetsuit!

description: This Private Day Tour provides the opportunity to explore the stunning landscape of the Yorkshire Dales National Park, visiting Bolton Castle, Askrigg (filming location for Darrowby in the BBC James Herriot 'All Creatures Great and Small' TV series), Kettlewell (main filming location for the Calendar Girls movie), The Oldest Sweetshop in the World at Pateley Bridge and the traditional Dales Market Town of Hawes, where you will have the opportunity to sample the cheeses at the award winning Wensleydale Creamery, the home of Yorkshire Wensleydale Cheese.

description:
Enjoy a classic guided half-day sea kayak trip in one of the UK’s premier sea-kayaking locations. Expert instructors teach you the skills you need so you can relax and paddle past dramatic cliffs, secret coves and wildlife-rich islands. Glide close to shore where larger boats cannot go, explore sea caves on higher tides, and pause on a remote sandy beach for a swim or a driftwood fire. Suitable for all ages and abilities with no prior experience required.

All safety and technical equipment

An experienced and highly qualified instructor

description:
A magical Christmas tour exploring London’s hidden and unusual lights and sights.

Tour around London’s hidden, unique and unusual Christmas lights & sights, whilst listening to our specially selected festive playlist of smash hits, Christmas classics and hilarious Christmas facts.

After a sell-out success last year, we are returning with our liberating and immersive, singing and dancing experience that has made us the number one walking tour in London - come along and blast away those winter blues!

Join us on this fun-packed tour around some of London’s hidden and unusual Christmas lights & sights, whilst listening (via our Silent Disco headsets) to our specially selected playlist consisting of some of the most popular smash hits, Christmas classics and of course our unrivalled hilarious commentary – all guaranteed to help you see in the New Year

name: The Chocoholic - London (formerly the Original Chocolate Making Workshop)
description:
Welcome to our classic workshop, The Chocoholic, an indulgent chocolate-making adventure filled with creativity and flair! Led by our charismatic chocolatiers, you’ll learn to craft fresh cream truffles using velvety dark chocolate ganache. Dip your creations into warm molten chocolate and decorate them with toppings like honeycomb and Maldon sea salt, all while enjoying a lively, hands-on experience.

You’ll also make custom chocolate bark, experimenting with marbling and intricate decoration techniques. When it’s time to take your treats home, you’ll have a box of delicious goodies to savour or share—though we won’t blame you if you keep them all for yourself!

Ready to step into a chocolate experience like no other? Book your spot now for a memorable, deliciously fun journey into the art of chocolate-making!

name: Private Windsor Town Tour
description:
Take Windsor at your own pace, spend quality time with a local and get your bearings on your very own private tour of Windsor.

A town steeped in royal heritage and set along the picturesque River Thames, Windsor is as charming as it is captivating. From its deep ties to the British monarchy to its eclectic architecture and vibrant modern life, there’s something for everyone to discover in this idyllic town.

Join your local expert guide for a 1.5-hour stroll through the main sights of Windsor, walking in the footsteps of kings, queens, and everyday folk. From William the Conqueror’s claim on the town after the Battle of 1066 to Queen Elizabeth II’s long-standing residence at Windsor Castle, history comes to life around every corner. Along the way, you'll not only uncover Windsor’s fascinating past but also gain insight into what it’s like to live and work in this historic town today.

Discover:

Windsor Guildhall
Windsor Castle

River Thames
Thames Street

& more you wouldn't discover yourself!

description:
About

Take Birmingham at your own pace with your own dedicated guide on this special 3 hour Birmingham City & Jewellery Quarter Tour. Choose any start time, and we can pick you up at your hotel in the city centre.

Immerse yourself in the sights and sounds of the city as you travel back through the centuries to discover how our city exploded from a small market town, to the second largest city in the UK.

On our longest private tour, see everything you need to see in Birmingham, from the key sights in the city centre to the historically important jewellery quarter area of the city.

You'll not only see and learn about our history, but you’ll also gain an authentic insight into modern life in the city today and explore some hidden gems along the way! From Birmingham’s boom during the industrial revolution, to our iconic canals, and of course, the home of Cadbury chocolate! There’s something for everyone on this unique experience.

name: Samuel Pepys's London Walk
description:
Samuel Pepys was more than just a diarist—he was a keen observer of 17th-century London, a naval administrator, and a man who lived through some of the city's most dramatic events. His vivid accounts provide an unparalleled glimpse into the Restoration era, covering everything from royal scandals and public executions to the Great Plague and the Great Fire.

What Can Customers Expect?
Step into Pepys’ London – Explore the city through the eyes of one of its most famous chroniclers.

Engaging Storytelling – Experience Pepys’ world brought to life by an expert guide, complete with entertaining anecdotes and historical insights.

Historic Landmarks – Walk through streets Pepys knew, visiting key locations tied to his life, including his final resting place (subject to availability).

Surprising Tales – Discover lesser-known aspects of Pepys’ life, from imprisonment and attempted mugging to encounters with exotic animals.

A Glimpse of 17th-Century Lo
highlights: Meeting Point – Arrive at Tower Hill. (exact meeting details provided upon booking).
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to check in and meet your guide. We leave on the dot.
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather-appropriate clothing, and a bottle of water. A notebook is optional for those who love jotting down historical facts!
Accessibility – Not Step-free. The route involves cobblestones. If you have any concerns, please get in touch before booking.
**Toilets & Refreshments – There are public toilet facilities located near,, in the underpass connecting Bridge Street and Parliament Street.
Start at Meeting Point – Introduction to Samuel Pepys and the historical context of 17th-century London.
The Great Fire of London – Visit locations associated with the fire and hear Pepys’ firsthand account of the disaster.
Pepys’ Home and Workplace – Explore the sites where he lived and worked, including his naval office.

Come and get your “Ticket to Ride” and explore Liverpool by bicycle, just as The Beatles did all those years ago.

We also travel through some of Liverpool’s beautiful parks which are inaccessible to cars, taxi’s, coaches and buses.

name: Full-Day Private Whitby & The North York Moors Tour
description: Spend three hours enjoying Whitby on this Private Day Tour from York or Harrogate, as well as exploring the scenic and picturesque North York Moors National Park. You will have a full three hours of free time in the fishing port of Whitby on this trip, to see Whitby Abbey (inspiration for the Dracula books), visit the Captain Cook Attractions, shop for some Whitby Jet or Gothic Fashion, sample Fish & Chips from the famous Magpie Café or just enjoy the beach, bustling port and waterfront – the choice is yours! As well as photo stops on the moors, you will also stop at the famous Kilburn White Horse, the market town of Helmsley (with its quaint cafes, shops and medieval castle) and the beautiful, traditional English villages of Goathland (Heartbeat and Harry Potter filming location) and Lealholm (with its stepping stones across the River Esk).

name: Half-Day Private North York Moors National Park Tour
description: This Private Tour provides the opportunity for you to get out into the countryside to enjoy the fresh air and scenic beauty of the North York Moors National Park. We will pick you up from your hotel (or other location of your choice) at 10.00am and head out of town for a circular tour of the National Park, with stops at Byland Abbey, Helmsley and the beautiful moorland village of Rosedale Abbey. There will also be photo stops along the way, including the highest point on the moors at the Lion Inn on Blakey Ridge. You will be able to buy food and drinks en route, but feel free to bring your own food along and we will stop somewhere suitable for you to have a picnic away from any crowds. The itinerary for this tour is flexible, talk to your Driver when you set off and we can make adjustments to suit your personal preferences. Departs 10.00am and drops off 2.30pm.

name: C.S. Lewis & J.R.R. Tolkien Walking Tour
description:
From earnest discussions of fantasy worlds in the Eagle and Child pub, to the first inspiration of Narnia in St Mary’s Passage, C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkien’s masterpieces are interwound with Oxford and its university.

Having both studied and taught at Oxford University, the first seeds of their most well-known and beloved works began here. Follow in the authors’ footsteps around their favourite Oxford haunts and hear the stories of their time at the university.

Operating on selected Saturdays, this tour is perfect for those literarily inclined, fans of The Chronicles of Narnia, Lord of the Rings and The Hobbit, and who want to learn more about these two greats of The Inklings Society, and see where they lived and learned.

name: The Anglo-Saxon World at Bamburgh and Lindisfarne
description:
The capital of Northumbria and the cradle of Christianity!
About

Since pre-history Bamburgh and Lindisfarne have had human settlements. As a fortress of the Kingdom of Northumbria Bamburgh was the centre of the largest Kingdom in England during the 7th and 8th centuries. In the middle ages, the Castle developed as an important fortress against Scottish attack - eventually developing into the impressive fortification you see today. Lindisfarne is famous for both the Lindisfarne Gospels and being one of the first locations in the United Kingdom to be attacked by the Vikings. It is called the cradle of English Christianity due to the importance of the monastery in the dark ages. Due to the tides, the tour may go to Lindisfarne at different times to enable safe crossing and return to the mainland. The Village of Bamburgh will also be included in the tour with St. Aiden’s Church and its Ossuary both being sights to visi

name: Rapid Rib Private Charter
description:
Looking for a private boat experience with your own crew? Charter our 8-metre Ribquestt RIB for an unforgettable ride along the Sussex coast. Seating up to 11 guests, this RIB is perfect for groups, special occasions, or just a unique day out on the water.

Equipped with a swim ladder for water access and a built-in sound system, you can dive in for a swim or cruise along to your favourite playlist. Whether you're planning a scenic run to the Seven Sisters Cliffs, a family adventure, or a birthday with a difference—this RIB delivers excitement, comfort, and unbeatable views.

Need space for a bigger group? You can also charter our Mediterranean RIB for extra seating. Just add both boats to your cart at checkout to enjoy a shared adventure on the water.

Use of onboard sound system

Swim ladder for water access

name: 剑桥校友伴你行 - 剑桥大学怪谈之旅（私人小团）
description:
剑桥已有 800 多年历史。在古老的学院建筑和狭窄的中世纪街巷之间，隐藏着和那些明信片宁静景色完全相反的故事。

我们的剑桥怪谈之旅是一场经过研究整理的夜间步行导览，带您探索真实记录的灵异传闻、历史悲剧以及在大学中流传数百年的传说。

讲解员由剑桥大学毕业校友或在读学生担任。

这并不是戏剧化的恐怖表演，没有道具、没有突然的惊吓，也没有浮夸的装扮。

相反，这是一场基于历史记录、民间传说以及学生代代相传故事的深入讲述，带你了解剑桥鲜为人知的一面。

当你穿过安静的学院街道和古老庭院时，将听到与剑桥多所历史学院相关的故事，例如：

圣体学院 Corpus Christi College 的神秘目击事件

在学生之间流传数百年的古老校园传说

剑桥中世纪时期较为黑暗的历史片段

我们还将探讨瘟疫爆发、处决、迷信与社会动荡如何影响学生与城镇居民的生活。

当夜幕降临、游客散去，剑桥呈现出完全不同的氛围。

建筑显得更加宏伟，街道更加安静，而这些故事也变得更加真实。

在冬季黑暗的夜晚体验尤其富有氛围——毕竟，这正是幽灵最容易出现的季节。

本行程为 户外步行导览，全程在室外进行，并以中文讲解。

What's included

独享一位剑桥校友作为您的导游，中文讲解

60 分钟步行导览

本地建议与推荐

What's not included

餐饮

小费

Itinerary

随后行程穿过古老学院街道与静谧小巷，讲述与以下地点相关的故事：

Clare College 克莱尔学院

name: 牛津校友伴你行 - 牛津大学步行导览 & 撑篙体验（私人小团）
description:
牛津是一座值得细细探索的城市，而真正理解它，需要同时从街道与河流两个角度体验。
这项 牛津步行导览与撑篙组合行程 将 90 分钟步行导览 与 River Cherwell 查韦尔河传统撑篙体验结合在一起，带来一次完整的牛津大学探索之旅。
行程首先从步行导览开始。
跟随牛津大学校友的脚步，由专业校友讲解员带领深入牛津大学核心区域。
这不仅仅是简单的景点讲解。
您将真正了解牛津大学今天如何运作，包括：
学院制度（Collegiate System）

导师制教学（Tutorial Method）

真实的申请与录取情况

学生生活与传统

在步行过程中，您将看到牛津最具代表性的地标，包括：
Radcliffe Camera 拉德克利夫图书馆

Bodleian Library 博德利图书馆

Sheldonian Theatre 谢尔登剧院

Bridge of Sighs 叹息桥

同时还会经过历史学院庭院、古老大门以及一些隐藏街角，感受牛津大学城市独特的氛围。
步行导览结束后，行程将在水上继续展开。
在 River Cherwell 切尔韦尔河 上乘坐传统撑篙船，缓缓经过 Christ Church Meadows 基督堂学院草坪 与多所学院花园，欣赏牛津最优美的自然景观。
这些河岸风景也与牛津文学历史紧密相连。例如：
Lewis Carroll 刘易斯·卡罗尔（《爱丽丝梦游仙境》作者）曾在 Christ Church 基督堂学院任教

Oscar Wilde 奥斯卡·王尔德 曾在 Magdalen College

name: Private Spanish • Oxford Walking & Punting Tour & Optional New College
description:
ONLINE RESERVATIONS RECOMMENDED

Duration
2.5 hours outdoor walking tour (New College not included)
3.5 hours partially indoor walking tour (New College included)

LIVE THE HISTORY

Take a fascinating walk through Oxford, where pivotal moments in history come alive. Explore landmarks like the lamp-post that inspired Narnia and the architecture that influenced His Dark Materials. Visit the meadows where Lewis Carroll first told the story of Alice in Wonderland, and marvel at some of the world's finest Gothic and Baroque architecture. Oxford is a city brimming with intriguing stories waiting to be discovered.

THE STUDENT’S STORY

Oxford's vibrant student life adds a unique touch to our tours. Our guides are either Oxford alumni or current students who blend their personal experiences with their passion for the city's history and architecture. They offer insights into student life, from BOPS to secret societies, and reveal which colleges are the oldest, the poshest, and the most quirky. Experience the true essence of Oxford.

Tailored to your needs and objectives, our private tour can be fully personalized to visit your specific places of interest. Alternatively, you can opt for our classic tour route.

Our c

name: Single Kayak Hire
description:
Kayaking is a beautiful way to explore Mounts Bay and get up close to St Michael's Mount. We offer kayaking rental by the hour in British made Tootega Sit-on top kayaks. Super stable and easy to paddle. We do have a designated area that you can kayak in and we do not allow our kayaks or boards to go around St Michael's Mount or out of sight of our beach base, but there is a whole lot of of wonderful water you can play in and crossing over to St Michael's Mount and exploring the Hogus reef on a kayak or SUP is a fantastic experience.
Children under 16 must be accompanied by an adult on the water.

British made Tootega sit-on-top kayaks

Wetsuits available to hire for £7

description:
Immerse yourself in one of our beautiful woodlands connecting as our ancestors did in the natural world with our hands on Nature Connections Rewilding Retreat Day designed to enhance a connection to nature, learn new skills, reconnect with yourself while reducing stress and improving wellbeing with engaging and transformative activities

Archery - connect to your inner focus and experience the ancient skill of archery. A primal skill that develops mindful concentration and experience a sense of self belief, achievement and of wellbeing.

Bushcraft: Fire lighting - a primal connection to nature’s resources. Master the fundamental skill of fire lighting safely by using natural materials. Experience the satisfaction of creating bushcraft fire and connecting with this primeval force. Ideal for discovering self-reliance, resourcefulness, and connecting with the elements.

name: School group: Private Secrets of Spitalfields Tour
description:
This tour reveals Spitalfields as a layered landscape of migration, work, religion and activism, from medieval foundations to Huguenot weavers, Jewish and Bangladeshi communities and today’s street art. Students see how one neighbourhood reflects global histories of persecution, refuge, labour and cultural change.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect
A rich case study in migration, urban change and community, ideal for History, Geography, RS, Sociology and Citizenship.​
First‑hand encounters with a Grade II‑listed former synagogue, Georgian weavers’ houses, a historic music hall and Brick Lane’s contemporary culture.​
Stories that illuminate concepts such as “refugee”, gentrification, religious diversity, labour organising and trade union history (including the match girls’ strike).​
Tour Reviews:
Patricia (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

From the moment of being greeted by Daniel, through to his concluding remarks, every word that he said was informative and of interest, whether as part of his plan or in response to questions from his audience. The walk was well paced and truly included secrets that even I, a long

Meeting Point – Liverpool Street Station (precise exit/meeting spot confirmed on booking to keep groups together safely).​
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early; this allows time for toilets, group organisation and moving away from busy station concourses.​
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, appropriate clothing for the weather, water, and optional worksheets for mapping migration waves and key turning points.​
Accessibility – The route includes cobbled streets, uneven pavements and some crossings; contact us to discuss adjustments or alternative stopping points.​

name: School group: Private Roman London Tour
description:
This tour uncovers Roman Londinium beneath the modern City, exploring walls, fortifications, streets and hidden remains that bring ancient urban life to life for students. Across 90 minutes, learners see how Rome founded, developed and then abandoned London, leaving traces still visible today.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect

A tightly focused introduction to Roman Britain ideal for KS2, KS3 and GCSE History, plus Classics and Archaeology taster sessions.​
Clear explanations of why the Romans chose this site, how the town functioned and why it declined, using physical evidence as a starting point.​
Opportunities to discuss continuity and change, evidence and interpretation, and how archaeologists reconstruct past societies.​

Tour Reviews:

Peter (5/5) - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
This was excellent and a great way to start getting out and about again. It was quite a revelation about the beginning of my home city. Our guide Ian was knowledgeable, enthusiastic, fri
highlights: Meeting Point – A clearly marked location in the City, with precise directions and a guide contact sent once the visit is confirmed.​
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early to allow time to gather the group and move away from busy junctions.​
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, suitable clothing, water, and optional clipboards for sketching plans of Londinium or noting key dates and terms.​
Accessibility – Some routes include cobbles, gradients and steps near old walls; where possible, alternatives can be offered with advance notice.​
Visit surviving stretches of Roman wall, gateways and fortifications, examining how they defended and defined Londinium.​
Explore sites that reveal streets, public buildings and everyday life, including trade, religion, entertainment and burial practices where visible.​

Consider the decline and “disappearance” of Roman London, and how later Londoners reused or built over Roman remains.​

After the Tour
End Location – Central City location near facilities and onward Roman or medieval sites.​

name: School group: Private City Highlights Tour
description:
This tour introduces students to two thousand years of history in the Square Mile, from Roman Londinium to the modern financial district. Learners see how the City has survived fire, plague, bombing and reform while remaining a global centre of trade and governance.​

What Teachers and Students Can Expect

An overview ideal for KS3–KS5 History, Geography, Citizenship, RE and Economics.​
Clear links to themes such as urban resilience, governance, finance, religion and empire using real streets and buildings as case studies.​
Opportunities to discuss how a small medieval city evolved into a world financial hub while retaining unique laws, customs and institutions.​

What's included

📍 Start: St Paul’s tube station (exact location confirmed on booking)

🏴 End: Within the Square Mile, near major transport and amenities

⏳ Duration: 90 minutes

🎓 Led by a qualified City of London guide experienced with school and university groups.​

What's not included

Gratuities

Itinerary
Before, During & After the Tour

Before the Tour

Meeting Point – A clearly identified landmark in the City, with detailed joining instructions sent in advance.​
Arrival Time
highlights: Meeting Point – A clearly identified landmark in the City, with detailed joining instructions sent in advance.​
Arrival Time – Please arrive 10 minutes early so students can be briefed and registered before the tour begins.​
What to Bring – Comfortable walking shoes, weather‑appropriate clothing, water and optional notebooks or worksheets.​
Accessibility – The route uses pavements and crossings and may include some gradients or cobbles; adaptations can usually be made with prior notice.​
Explore major landmarks such as St Paul’s Cathedral, the Bank of England, the London Mithraeum and Guildhall (exteriors), as well as lesser‑known alleyways and courtyards.​
Hear stories of the Great Fire, the Blitz, medieval guilds, financial innovation and the City’s unusual system of government and ceremony.​
Discuss how religion, commerce, empire and modern finance intersect in the Square Mile and what this means for London and the wider UK.​
End Location– Central City site with nearby toilets, cafés and coach pick‑up options.​

name: Festival of Britain Private Tour
description: Step back to 1951 and experience the Festival of Britain as thousands did in its heyday. Explore the Southbank’s transformed landscape and hear how the Festival symbolised hope, innovation, and national recovery following the austerity of the post-war period. Learn about the architectural marvels, exhibitions, and whimsical attractions including the Skylon, Dome of Discovery, Land of Britain, and the “Tempest Prognosticator.” Discover restored artwork, iconic Coade stone lions, and the stories behind the Festival’s music, including the West Indian steel drum bands and Festival Calypso. This walk brings to life both the excitement and challenges of one of Britain’s

name: Whitby & The North York Moors
description:
Spend three hours enjoying Whitby on our friendly, small-group, Guided Day Trip from York, as well as exploring the scenic and picturesque North York Moors National Park. You will have a full three hours of free time in the fishing port of Whitby on this trip, to see Whitby Abbey (inspiration for the Dracula books), visit the Captain Cook Attractions, shop for some Whitby Jet or Gothic Fashion, sample Fish & Chips from the famous Magpie Café or just enjoy the bustling port and waterfront – the choice is yours! As well as photo stops on the moors, you will also stop at the famous Kilburn White Horse, the market town of Helmsley (with its quaint cafes, shops and medieval castle) and the beautiful, traditional English villages of Goathland (Harry Potter and Heartbeat filming location) and enjoy the stunning views at the Hole of Horcum.

description:
Experience the thrill of driving an agile 'sports tank' at SWB Motorsport in Dunchurch near Rugby. Since 2014, we've hosted military vehicle adventures, and now you can take control of the powerful Alvis CVR(T) Spartan. With a speed over 50mph, rapid steering and a very clever, 7 speed gearbox, this 8-ton beast offers an unforgettable ride. Ideal for adrenaline junkies, this session combines performance, precision, and fun. The experience lasts approximately 20 minutes and is perfect for those seeking a unique, hands-on military vehicle challenge. Join us for an unforgettable driving adventure in a legendary light tank.

Pay & Play days offer free roam riding across our 100-acre trials venue, giving riders the freedom to explore and ride at their own pace.

Riders can enjoy a wide variety of natural trials terrain, including streams, bankings, hill climbs, flat land and rocky sections. Our purpose-built Red Bull Trials Park is also available, featuring a variety of sections spread across three levels, with something suitable for all abilities.

This option is strictly for riders bringing their own trials bike and is ideal for those who want unstructured riding time rather than coached training.

description: We will pick you up from your hotel (or other location of your choice) in York, Harrogate or Thirsk at 10.00am and take you on a scenic drive through the North York Moors National Park, following which you will have about two hours free time to enjoy the seaside town of Whitby, before the return journey. You can see Whitby Abbey (inspiration for the Dracula books), visit the Captain Cook Attractions, shop for some Whitby Jet or Gothic Fashion, sample Fish & Chips from the famous Magpie Café or just explore the beach, fishing port and waterfront – the choice is yours!

name: Cruise and Dine at The Folly Restaurant
description:
Elegance and simplicity come together in this river cruise and three-course dining experience at The Folly

Your experience begins boarding one of our elegant Edwardian Gentleman's River Launches for a 45-minute cruise taking in the highlights of river life in Oxford. We see Folly Bridge, Christchurch Meadows, the University boathouse, and part of the Regatta course whilst a complementary glass of Prosecco* is served.

We disembark at The Folly restaurant where your exquisite meal will be served in charming surroundings either inside the restaurant or, if the weather allows, on the floating river terrace. The Folly's a la carte menu offers relaxed fine dining (sample menu here) and there is a comprehensive wine list available to complement your meal.**

Our Cruise and Dine combines two of Oxford's great attractions to create an exceptional and memorable experience.

More info

Ticket price includes a starter, main course, and dessert from The Folly a la carte menu. A surcharge may apply to a small number of premium menu items.

**Additional items such as drinks to accompany your meal, side orders, and service charge are not included and must be paid to The Folly at the end of your meal.

name: Jungle Hunt
description:
2 Yelverton Road, Bournamouth, BH1 1EF

£26 Per Player • 2-7 Players • Ages 8+

	501 Reviewers
#5 of 68 Fun & Games in Bournemouth
TripAdvisor Traveler Rating
According to TripAdvisor travelers as of April 2026
Can you save the day before being trapped in the jungle?!
Duration

60 minutes

Rates

£26 Per Player

About

In the depths of the jungle, two tribes have been at war for a very long time, the Watchati tribe and the Watchatchu tribe.

To settle their differences they have decided to marry off the prince and the princess from either tribe. They need the help of a sacred white bat, the ‘shikaka’, which is an important part of the ceremony. Unfortunately, someone has kidnapped the bat!

You must hunt down and rescue the bat in time for the wedding or risk being trapped in the jungle while war breaks out!

The wedding is in one hour…

The game is suitable for adults, families and children. Children under 8 years old must be accompanied by an adult. Children under 5 years old are free.
